This position is available immediately through the end of the school year and will resume following summer break. This is a 40 hour per week position working 42 weeks per year.

        The selected candidate provides physical examinations, health screenings, treatment for minor illnesses and injuries, immunizations, reproductive health, and health education to adolescent students and works closely with the school nurse to treat students and keep them in school.  The APC works with a multidisciplinary staff at the center to address critical issues that children face guiding them towards productive and healthy lives and in doing so improving the health of our communities. Don't miss this great opportunity to support our community and have a work/life balance!

        ]]> ChristianaCare is looking for a Medical Assistant I for our UroGyn office.

        Urogynecology is located at 4735 Ogletown Stanton Rd #1208, Newark, DE 19713. It is a specialized field focused on diagnosing and treating pelvic floor disorders affecting the bladder, uterus, vagina, and rectum. Our board‑certified physicians— the only urogynecologists in Delaware certified in Female Pelvic Medicine and Reconstructive Surgery—provide comprehensive care using physical therapy, medications, and surgical options ranging from minimally invasive to complex procedures. Patients may also have access to clinical research trials and innovative treatments

        Urogynecology | ChristianaCare


        Work Schedule:

        • Monday-Friday, 8:00am- 4:30pm

        • Monday and Wednesdays are in the Smyrna location

        • Tuesday, Thursday, and Fridays in the Newark location

        Why You’ll Love Working at ChristianaCare

        • Comprehensive, First‑Day Health Coverage:
          Comprehensive first‑day coverage, including medical, dental, vision, and life insurance. Caregivers earning under $50,000 pay $0 in paycheck premiums for medical and prescription coverage. Enjoy low deductibles, plus $0 copays for unlimited primary care (in‑office or virtual) with ChristianaCare providers and unlimited urgent care visits at GoHealth facilities.

        • Generous Paid Time Off
          Enjoy 32 days of PTO each year, including 7 major holidays, with options to roll over or cash out unused time.

        • Tuition Reimbursement for Career Growth
          Advance your education with up to $5,250 per year in tuition reimbursement.

        • Grow Your Family with Confidence
          We’re here to support every step of your journey to parenthood—whatever that looks like for you. From advanced fertility care through Progyny to free maternity education and support, plus up to $5,000 in adoption assistance, our benefits are designed to help you build your family with confidence and care.

        • Paid Parental Leave
          Receive 12 weeks of paid parental leave to support your growing family, after one year of service.

        • Retirement Planning & Financial Well-Being
          Access two retirement plans, including a 403(b) with up to a 50% match and a company-funded contribution of 3%–5% based on service. Plus, take advantage of free financial coaching, counseling, and budgeting support.

        Duties / Responsibilities:

        The Medical Assistant I greets and supports patients virtually and in person, prepares them for visits, performs basic clinical tasks (e.g., testing, injections, EKGs), assists with exams, and maintains exam rooms and equipment. They manage EMR updates, patient communications, referrals, authorizations, and scheduling, while handling clerical duties, troubleshooting video visits, and supporting patient portal access.

        Position Qualifications:

        • High School Graduate or GED required.

        • A graduate of a recognized Medical Assistant Program or hold current Medical Assistant Certification by a national organization accredited by the National Commission for Certifying Agencies (NCCA) or the American National Standards Institute (ANSI).

        Why ChristianaCare
        Do you want to make a meaningful impact in people’s lives? As a Medical Assistant I, you’ll play an essential role in providing high-quality care and supporting better patient outcomes each day. This is more than a job—it’s a chance to be part of a mission-driven team focused on improving healthcare.

        When we lead with love, excellence is inevitable.
        #LI-EH1

          Advance your education with up to $5,250 per year in tuition reimbursement.
        • Grow Your Family with Confidence
          We’re here to support every step of your journey to parenthood—whatever that looks like for you. From advanced fertility care through Progyny to free maternity education and support, plus up to $5,000 in adoption assistance, our benefits are designed to help you build your family with confidence and care.
        • Paid Parental Leave
          Receive 12 weeks of paid parental leave to support your growing family, after one year of service.
        • Retirement Planning & Financial Well-Being
          ​Access two retirement plans, including a 403(b) with up to a 50% match and a company-funded contribution of 3%–5% based on service. Plus, take advantage of free financial coaching, counseling, and budgeting support.

        Duties & Responsibilities: 

        The Medical Assistant II supports virtual and on-site patient care by rooming patients, recording vitals, performing tests, injections, medication administration, and EKGs. They assist clinicians with exams and procedures, prepare treatment rooms, manage EMR documentation, handle patient callbacks, and maintain equipment sterilization. Additional duties include managing authorizations, referrals, refills, scheduling, quality control of POCT equipment, clerical tasks, supply management, staff coverage, and ensuring safe work practices.

        Position Qualifications:

        • High School Graduate or GED required.

        • A graduate of a recognized Medical Assistant Program and hold current Medical Assistant Certification by a national organization accredited by the National Commission for Certifying Agencies (NCCA) or the American National Standards Institute (ANSI) or obtain within three months of hire.

        • Two years’ experience as a Medical Assistant, is required, three years’ is preferred.
           

        Why ChristianaCare
        Do you want to make a meaningful impact in people’s lives? As a Medical Assistant II, you’ll play an essential role in providing high-quality care and supporting better patient outcomes each day. This is more than a job—it’s a chance to be part of a mission-driven team focused on improving healthcare.

        When we lead with love, excellence is inevitable.
        #LI-HB1

        Hourly Pay Range: $19.84 - $29.76

        This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

        ]]> Care Coordinator II

        Department: Center for Surgical Optimization
        Schedule: Day Shift | full-time | Monday–Friday | No Weekends | No Holidays

        Location: Newark 7E

        Advance your nursing career in a role designed for clinical excellence, autonomy, and long‑term professional growth. The Care Coordinator II within our Center for Surgical Optimization offers an opportunity for experienced nurses to apply advanced clinical knowledge while enjoying a predictable, weekday schedule—free from nights, weekends, and holidays.

        Position Overview

        The Care Coordinator II plays a key role in managing and optimizing care for patients undergoing joint replacement and spine surgery. This position partners closely with surgeons, primary care providers, interdisciplinary teams, and family members to ensure coordinated, evidence‑based care across the full continuum—from pre‑operative planning through post‑acute transitions.

        This is an exempt position with 8‑hour day shifts, with start times between 7:00–8:00 AM based on patient census.

        ChristianaCare Benefits

        ChristianaCare invests in you—professionally and personally.

        • Medical, dental, and vision coverage from day one

        • Generous PTO with a PTO pay‑out option

        • Care.com membership and backup dependent care through Care@Work

        • Tuition assistance, with pre‑paid tuition for select nursing programs

        • Retirement planning, financial mentoring, plus 403(b) and defined contribution plans

        • On‑site gym access and fitness & wellness reimbursement

        • Exclusive employee discounts on travel, entertainment, and more

        Benefits designed to support your life, career, and future.

        Responsibilities:

        • Completes and documents a preoperative health assessment screening and perioperative history and assessment for patients scheduled for joint replacement or spine surgery according to established criteria.

        • Reviews clinical documentation and communicates findings with providers of the CSO Team, 7S/7E and attending physician/anesthesia team and revises plan of care as needed based on findings.

        • Demonstrates skills and knowledge necessary to provide care to adolescent, adult and geriatric populations, including knowledge of growth and development, the ability to obtain and interpret information to identify patient needs and to provide the appropriate care needed.

        • Assesses patient/family learning needs/barriers and carries out teaching measures and makes all appropriate referrals utilizing available resources.

        • Completes follow-up phone calls for patient discharge and reports on findings in electronic medical records, contacting surgeons with questions or concerns.

        • Demonstrates ability to prioritize workflow to complete tasks.

        • Establishes methods that support reciprocal communication between physician offices, lab services, specialty services, 7S/7Eand the CSO team.

        • Assesses patient post discharge care needs, initiates plan of care, and initiates inclusion of the necessary interdisciplinary team members.

        • Case Management duties prior to surgery include pre-referrals in Ensocare for Home Health Care (HHC) / infusion services, lab draws, durable medical equipment such as hospital beds, walkers, commodes, home oxygen, and transportation home

        •  Inpatient includes:

        • Facilitates daily and as needed multidisciplinary rounds, team and family meetings, rounds daily on 7S/7E ensuring plan for discharge matches pre-operative assessment and making necessary changes as appropriate supportive of the joint/spine program to HHC/physical therapy inpatient in home or outpatient. Ongoing updates for 7S/7E include DME floor stock, and communication tool used by charge nurse and Nurse Practitioner.

        • Case Management support post-discharge includes:

        • Scripted calls after discharge and appropriate follow-up with surgeon’s office to secure prescriptions needed and any follow-up appointments. .  Care feedback coordinated with Program and Unit Manager as needed.

        • Participates in system-wide care management and other program activities as requested. This includes rounding on 7S joint replacement patients/7Eand CSA spine patients for discharge preparedness, multi-disciplinary rounds and updating tracking forms.

        • Maintains collaborative professional working relationships with clinicians and administrative staff necessary to develop and implement a successful cross continuum care management for patients undergoing joint replacement /spine surgery.

        • Collaborates with Total Joint Replacement/Spine Program Manager on ongoing performance improvement initiatives.

        • Demonstrates understanding and application of CCHS values and behaviors in daily work.

        • Serves as a patient advocate when barriers impede progress and/or collaboration between practitioners involved in care that is fragmented.

        • Performs patient education via telephone or in person using teach back principals; collaborates with patient and family to establish realistic goals and activities to enhance patient self-management and participation in plan of care through discharge.

        • Use theoretical and educational frameworks and expert clinical knowledge base to promote, develop and provide patient and family education.

        • Assumes responsibility for personal growth and is willing to share knowledge with co-workers, management, physicians and other healthcare providers.

        • Utilizes effective verbal and written communication skills with patients, families, co-workers, management, physicians, and other members of the healthcare team.

        EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

        • BSN is required.  RN, licensed to practice nursing in the state of Delaware

        • BLS required

        • Master’s degree in nursing preferred

        • Three or more years of clinical experience in acute care or Peri anesthesia care required

        • Relevant, direct orthopedic/spine experience as a professional nurse

        Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

        ]]> Christiana Care is currently seeking a Registered Nurse to work on 7E. This Surgical Spine and knee and hip replacement unit offers a unique and exciting specialty consisting of a variety of patient populations and experiences. ChristianaCare offers one of the most advanced, cutting-edge spine care programs in the United States. Through our experience and ongoing research, we continue to advance the standard of care to provide the best outcomes for all our spine by providing post operative care. The program includes a 30-bed unit within Christiana Hospital that provides comprehensive care for people suffering from cervical, thoracic and lumbar spine disorders. Conditions treated include degenerative diseases, herniated discs, spinal stenosis, tumors and spinal-cord injuries. Registered Nurses work with PAs and has a dedicated Nurse Practitioner.

        Highlights:

        • Growth Opportunities defined by our Clinical Ladder.
        • Generous PTO, Competitive pay with shift differentials & Robust Benefits Package.

        • Prefers at least 1-2 years med/surgical experience.

        This is a full time flex position (72 per pay). Hours are Day/Midnight rotating 12-hour shifts. Shifts will rotate between 7 a.m. - 7 p.m. and 7 p.m.- 7 a.m.  Requirements include a weekend commitment based upon tenure with ChristianaCare and 2 holidays per year.

        The emergency departments at ChristianaCare’s Christiana Hospital, Wilmington Hospital, Union Hospital, and the free-standing emergency department in Middletown, Delaware treat a combined total of more than 225,000 patients each year, making our doctors, nurses and support staff among the most experienced emergency medical professionals in the region.  Union Hospital Emergency Department on our Cecil Campus is a 27 bed Emergency Department, in Cecil County Maryland.  Come Join Our Team!  ChristianaCare, Union Hospital is committed to ensuring our patients have the highest quality of care.

        ChristianaCare Offers:

        • Growth opportunities defined by our clinical ladder!
        • Full Medical, Dental, Vision effective day one.
        •  Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions, plus life insurance.
        • Generous paid time off with annual roll-over and opportunities to cash out

Our nurses work closely with providers to provide excellent quality of care and patient safety.

        ]]> ChristianaCare, a nationally recognized leader in healthcare, is actively recruiting a BE/BC Pathologist to join the Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine in Newark, DE.  

        The successful candidate will join a stable, close-knit team of 10 physicians with varying surgical pathology and cytology subspecialty interests, and 6 Pathologists’ Assistants who perform surgical and autopsy grossing duties.

        Highlights:

        • Well-rounded, predominantly adult centered case mix, including a variety of interesting cancer and non-cancer related cases

        • Affiliated NCI selected Cancer Center and Research Institute allows for many collaborative opportunities, including several multi-disciplinary conferences

        • In-house immunohistochemistry with a broad and evolving menu of over 150 antibodies (including multiplex capabilities), and in-house flow cytometry

        • Must have the ability to be licensed in the state of Delaware and meet credentialing requirements

        Compensation & benefits package include:

        • Eligible for Federal Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF) program

        • Generous Time Away from Practice (TAP) program for CME, planned, and unplanned time off.

        • Fully paid malpractice insurance

        • 12 weeks paid protected parental leave

        • Employee wellness program with on-site fitness facilities and classes, and a variety of membership and discount options


        About ChristianaCare:
        ChristianaCare is one of the country's most dynamic health systems, centered on improving health outcomes, making high-quality care more accessible and lowering health care costs. ChristianaCare includes an extensive network of outpatient services, home health care, medical aid units, three hospitals (1,300 beds), a Level I trauma center and a Level III neonatal intensive care unit, a comprehensive stroke center and regional centers of excellence in heart and vascular care, cancer care and women's health. We rank 21st in the nation for hospital admissions and 6th for admission of stroke patients.

        ChristianaCare is a not-for-profit teaching health system with more than 290 residents and fellows. We are continually ranked by US News & World Report as a Best Hospital. With our unique, data-powered care coordination service and a focus on population health and value-based care, ChristianaCare is shaping the future of health care.

        Working for ChristianaCare offers the best of urban and suburban living with multiple housing options, excellent restaurants and entertainment venues, quality public and private schools, and access to some of the nation’s best colleges and universities. Located two hours (or less) from beautiful beaches in Delaware, Maryland, and New Jersey, as well as popular ski resorts in the Pocono Mountains. The location provides easy access to Washington, DC, Philadelphia, and New York City, including several affiliated international airports - all within a short drive or train ride from Newark, DE.

        Annual Compensation Range $342,253.00 - $512,964.00

The actual compensation within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on a variety of factors, including without limitation the fair market value for physician services in the relevant specialty and the candidate’s relevant experience, education, training, credentials, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements. The compensation range listed may encompass various forms of applicable compensation for this position, including, but not limited to, productivity incentives, value-based incentives and other ancillary forms of compensation dependent upon operational factors such as hours worked, call coverage and other factors. ChristianaCare will offer compensation at an appropriate point within the above range or, less frequently, may offer a level of compensation outside the listed range as warranted by the circumstances.

        ]]> Registered Nurse (RN) - Acute Care Rehabilitation

        FT Night Shift (Hrs: 7p-7a) Weekends & holidays per department guidelines.

        Wilmington, DE

        ChristianaCare's Acute Care Rehabilitation Center at Wilmington Hospital is growing and looking for an experienced Registered Nurse (RN).

        Rehabilitation Services at ChristianaCare Wilmington Hospital helps our patients reclaim their independence and activity level, putting them back on the road to their best health. We are an accredited Comprehensive Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility with specialty accreditation in stroke, brain injury, and limb loss by the Commission on Accreditation of Rehabilitation Facilities (CARF).

        Our team of specially trained healthcare professionals at the ChristianaCare Center for Rehabilitation at Wilmington Hospital (CRWH) is dedicated to rehabilitation to help patients regain or improve the skills needed for daily life. Patients receive nursing care, the medical staff manages medical problems, and rehabilitation is provided in our state-of-the-art center. We are internationally recognized and accredited as a Comprehensive Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility with specialty accreditations in stroke, brain injury, and amputation by the Commission on Accreditation of Rehabilitation Facilities (CARF).

        ChristianaCare Offers:

        • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.
        • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions.
        • Generous paid time off with annual rollover and opportunities to cash out.
        • 12-week paid parental leave.

        • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to Care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial mentor, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets, and much more!

        Education & Experience Requirements:

        • Delaware RN License or Compact Nursing License required.
        • Associate Degree in Nursing (ADN) required. Applicants must commit to obtaining a BSN within three years of the hire date.  
        • Minimum of one year of RN experience required.  
        • Preferred Experience: 1-2 years in Rehabilitation, Med/Surg, or Stepdown.
        • Basic Life Support (BLS) certification required.

        ChristianaCare Earns Third Consecutive Apex Award for Excellence in Respiratory Care.

        ChristianaCare is currently seeking  Respiratory Therapists to work Full time, 72 hours on 3rd shift/overnight.  Hours for the position are 6:30pm -7am.  Every 3rd weekend and holiday required.  This position is at Christiana Hospital with rotations to Wilmington Hospital.

        The Respiratory Care Department provides service for the Christiana and Wilmington hospitals. Respiratory Therapists provide service to all areas of the hospital as part of a specialized team including adult and infant critical care units, medical-surgical floors and emergency care.

        There are approximately one hundred and fifty six full and part time therapists working throughout Christiana Care Health System. There are opportunities to become a member of one of the most involved departments in innovative therapy and research in a respected environment where you are valued as part of the multidisciplinary team.

        Benefits of working at Christiana Care:

        • You will work in a role that directly impacts the lives of our patients, staff & community!

        • Generous PTO, Competitive Pay & Robust Benefits Package.

        • 12 weeks Paid Parental Leave

        • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

        • We also provide tuition assistance for degreed programs. Our medical/dental/vision benefits are available to our caregivers on their first day of employment! And we offer two different mechanisms toward planning for your retirement, a 403(b) and a defined contribution plan.

        EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:
        • Graduate of a CoArc approved School of Respiratory Therapy
        • Respiratory Licensure in the state of practice required (Delaware,

        ]]> Sterile Processing Technician (CRCST or CSPDT REQUIRED)

        Casual/Per Diem

        (2) 5 hours shifts during the week- evening/overnight weeknight shift | 10pm-3am Tues, Wed or Wed, Thursday

        (2) 8 hours shifts every other weekend days with open flexibility for all shifts |Every other weekend 9a-5:30pm

        Location: Wilmington

        At ChristianaCare, excellence in patient care begins long before a procedure starts. We are seeking a Sterile Processing Technician to ensure surgical instruments and equipment are cleaned, assembled, packaged, and sterilized to support patient safety and clinical excellence.

        This role performs instrument processing in compliance with CBSPD, AAMI, and HSPA standards while following evidence‑based practices and safety policies related to infection prevention and bloodborne pathogens. Technicians maintain knowledge of aseptic technique and basic microbiology and participate in ongoing training to stay current with evolving sterilization practices.

        RESPONSIBILITIES:

        Cleans and decontaminates surgical instruments and other medical equipment following established policy and procedure.

        Inspects, assembles, packages and sterilizes medical devices following manufacturer’s recommendations and established procedure.

        Assemble, wrap and label basic and especially specialty instruments and sets according to information in the CensiTrfac system for tracking purposes.

        Receive, unload, sort, inspect, wash and process instruments and equipment for decontamination both manually and by automated means.

        Reads, interprets, and records sterilization parameters from sterilizer recording mechanism and completes records of sterilization process, chemical/biological test results, and sterilizer cleaning.

        Provides asset management and support of surgical instruments and trays belonging to or consigned to the facility, in accordance with The Department of Health, TJC, OSHA, CDC, AAMI and AORN standards.

        Wrap and label stainless steel items.

        Operate and perform daily cleaning and maintenance of all departmental equipment per policies for multiple sterilization and decontamination equipment.

        Inventory and restock reprocessed sterile items in sterile storage areas.

        Fill Operating Room case cart requisitions for scheduled next day cases, add-on cases and STAT cases with instruments, linen, stainless steel items, soft good supplies, etc.

        Unload washer-disinfector racks of decontaminated instruments and stainless steel items and place in designated areas.

        Assist with orientation and training of Sterile Processing Interns

        Maintain assigned work area in a clean and organized manner following written departmental housekeeping policy.

        Answer telephone calls as appropriate.

        EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

        High School Diploma or GED required.

        Sterile Processing Technician Certification CBSPD/CRCST required. Individuals that were employed prior to requirements will be grandfathered.

        Hourly Pay Range: $19.84 - $29.76

        This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

        ChristianaCare is currently seeking a per diem Occupational Therapist to work with a dynamic team. This is a weekend Per Diem position and will be at least two weekend days per month with the opportunity to pick up weekday shifts.

        The selected candidate will work caring for patients in the acute Rehab Unit located at Wilmington Hospital. In this setting, patients are seen primarily in our well-equipped, state of the art therapy gym, and our Transitional Living Apartment with access to a fully equipped kitchen, bathroom and living room/bedroom set up.

        This exciting position allows the selected candidate to see a large variety of patient populations including Neuro, Trauma, Cardiac, and General Debility while working with a supportive team of OTs. The OT experience at ChristianaCare offers a strong team approach and frequent interdisciplinary interaction.

        • At least one year of clinical experience preferred

        • AOTA preferred
        • New graduates will be considered and are encouraged to apply

        This is a flat rate position, any individual hired into this position will be paid $59.00/hour plus any applicable differentials.

        ]]> PRIMARY FUNCTION:

        To prepare patients for invasive procedure, and assist the physician during a diagnostic catheterization, PTCA, or electrophysiology study.  Rotates through all aspects of the laboratory.

        PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

        • Responsible for providing clinical for electrophysiology procedures including, but not limited to Pacemaker and defibrillator implants, EP studies, ablations, and loops recorders. 

        • Responsible for providing clinical for cardiac interventional procedures including, but not limited to Diagnostic Catheterization, Balloon Angioplasty, Peripheral Vascular Angioplasty, Rotational and Directional atherectomy, Stent Placement, Cardiac Structural Interventions (TAVR, EVAR. Mitral Clip, and Watchman), as well as electro-physiology procedures.

        • Performs duties of a scrub assistant during a procedure, including controlling and manipulating guide wires and diagnostic catheters, and maintaining a sterile field.  

        • Responsible for assisting RN in provision of safe and effective patient care.  

        • Ensures proper safety standards are met for compliance with departmental policies, radiation safety standards, and joint commission regulations.  

        • Operates required imaging equipment using established guidelines and imaging protocols. 

        • Implements daily and weekly QA and maintenance procedures on all specified cardiac Cath equipment according to established policies and procedures. 

        • Monitors the patient throughout the interventional procedure.  

        • Monitors and records intracardiac and intravascular pressures.

        • Anticipates the activities of each existing Electrophysiology and/or Cath lab and keeps the schedule flowing consistently.  

        • Maintains proper customer services with all areas that interact with laboratory.  

        • Keeps a neat and clean work environment.  

        • Instructs students and residents and performs preceptor duties when necessary.  

        • Demonstrates skills and knowledge necessary to provide care appropriate to adolescent, adult, and geriatric patients, including knowledge of growth and development, the ability to obtain and interpret information to identify patient needs, and to provide the care needed. 

        • Maintains patient confidentiality  

        • Consistently uses two patient identifiers

        • Completes all mandatory education  

        •  Performs other related duties as required

        • Demonstrates compliance with ChristianaCare and departmental policies and procedures.

        • Displays actions in accordance with ChristianaCare Mission Statement and Core Values

        • Performs assigned work safely, adhering to established departmental safety rules and practices.

        • Reports to supervisor, in a timely manner, any unsafe activities, conditions, hazards, or safety violations that may cause injury to oneself, other employees, patients and visitors.

        • Performs other duties as required.

        SCHEDULE:

        Shift - Day Shift 7am-5:30pm M-F 

        No weekends or holidays. 

        EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

        • Completion of registered X-Ray Technologist or Cardiovascular Technologist program. 

        • Cardiac Catheterization experience preferred.

        • An equivalent combination of education and experience may be substituted.

        SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS:

        BLS and ACLS within one year of hire.

        SCOPE, PURPOSE, AND FREQUENCY OF CONTACTS:

        Frequent contact with physicians, nurses, allied health personnel, patients, visitors, both internal and external. 

        DIRECTION/SUPERVISION OF OTHERS:

         Students from Delaware Technical College, Pennsylvania College of Health Sciences, and Thomas Jefferson     University

        DIRECTION/SUPERVISION RECEIVED:

        Manager, Heart and Vascular Interventional Labs

         Assistant Manager or Assistant Nurse Manager, Electrophysiology Lab Manager, Heart and Vascular   Interventional Labs Director, Heart and Vascular Interventional Services Vice President, Heart and Vascular  Medical Director, Heart and Vascular Interventional Services  

        EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

        • Completion of registered X-Ray Technologist or Cardiovascular Technologist program (RCES or RCIS).  

        • Associate or bachelor’s degree with training in Cardiovascular technology.

        • Register Cardiac Electrophysiology Specialist (RCES) preferred.

        • At least two (2) years’ experience required

        KNOWLEDGE, SKILL, AND ABILITY REQUIREMENTS:

        • Knowledge of the anatomy and physiology of the heart.

        • Knowledge of rhythms, dysrhythmia, and pressures in the chambers of the heart.

        • Knowledge of policies and procedures, knowledge of the intro-aortic balloon pump.

        • Knowledge of drug interactions as related to pediatric, adolescent, adult and geriatric patients.

        • Knowledge of radiation safety for patients and employees.

        • Skill in manual dexterity and viewing a computer terminal.

        • Skill in communicating professionally on any level of contact.

        • Skill in oral and written communications.

        • Ability to calculate valve areas.

        • Ability to maintain an efficient productivity level.

        • Ability to perform CPR if needed on any individual.

        SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS:

        BLS

        ACLS within one year of hire. 

        PHYSICAL DEMANDS:

        Frequent lifting of incapacitated patients, standing and bending for extended periods while wearing a protective lead apron (weighs 20 lbs.).  

        Pushing/Pulling of patients on stretchers  

        WORKING CONDITIONS:

        Limited exposure to radiation, confined spaces, and blood borne pathogens/body fluids.  

        Exposure to potential for cuts and/or punctures, and repetitive motion tasks.  

        Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

        This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

        ]]> ChristianaCare, a leading healthcare system in Delaware, is seeking an experienced Colorectal Surgeon to join our well-established team. This exciting opportunity offers a collaborative and innovative environment with a focus on advanced colorectal care, including robotic and minimally invasive surgery, in a state-of-the-art facility, including a new office specifically designed and built for colon and rectal surgery.  

        This position offers the ideal opportunity to

        • Join a highly skilled and experienced team of 5 board certified colorectal surgeons.
        • Contribute to student education and surgical resident training and research through our ACGME-accredited General Surgery Residency with 24/7 coverage by resident staff
        • Practice 100% Colorectal Surgery position, no general surgery.
        • Clinical practice in Newark and Wilmington and plans to expand to southern Delaware.
        • Utilize advanced technology: ChristianaCare uses the latest technology with Robotic surgery as well as Endoscopy and Anorectal procedures.

        Position Qualifications

        • BC/BE American Board of Surgery and American Board of Colon and Rectal Surgery
        • Must have the ability to be licensed in the State of Delaware and meet credentialing requirements
        • Robotic and anorectal experience required. Prior experience preferred.
        • IBD experience, Research/Teaching a plus

        Responsibilities

        • Practice full spectrum of elective and emergent colorectal procedures and adhere to established hospital policies, procedures, objectives, QI initiatives and relevant protocols.
        • Contribute to medical student, resident, and post-graduate teaching.
        • Participate in Colorectal Surgery Call schedule as assigned (no general surgery)

        Compensation and Benefits

        • Competitive base salary, annual bonus incentives, loan repayment eligibility for Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF), plus sign-on bonus
        • Health, dental and vision benefits.
        • Generous Time Away from Practice (TAP) program for CME, planned, and unplanned time off.
        • 403(b) (with company match), 457b and Defined Contribution Plans
        • Fully paid malpractice insurance with tail coverage and 12 weeks paid parental leave.
        • Robust employee wellness program including a variety of membership and discount options.

        About ChristianaCare

        Headquartered in Wilmington, Delaware, ChristianaCare is one of the country's most dynamic health care organizations! ChristianaCare includes an extensive network of primary care and outpatient services, home health care, urgent care centers, three hospitals (1,336 beds), a freestanding emergency department, a Level I trauma center and a Level III neonatal intensive care unit, a comprehensive stroke center and regional centers of excellence in heart and vascular care, cancer care and women's health.

        ChristianaCare is rated by Healthgrades as one of America's 50 Best Hospitals and continually ranked among the nation's best by U.S. News & World Report, and Newsweek.

        Connected by Amtrak and I-95 this region is a hub for excellent restaurants, entertainment and sporting venues, cultural events and some of the nation's best colleges and universities, our location puts you within 1-3 hours of four international airports, major cities including Philadelphia, New York City and Washington, D.C., popular beach towns in MD, DE and NJ and popular ski resorts in PA and NY.

        #LI-YM1

        Annual Compensation Range $382,211.00 - $583,007.00

        The above compensation range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting for full-time hours in the respective specialty. The actual compensation within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on a variety of factors, including without limitation the fair market value for physician services in the relevant specialty and the candidate’s relevant experience, education, training, credentials, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements. The compensation range listed may encompass various forms of applicable compensation for this position, including, but not limited to, productivity incentives, value-based incentives and other ancillary forms of compensation dependent upon operational factors such as hours worked, call coverage and other factors. ChristianaCare will offer compensation at an appropriate point within the above range or, less frequently, may offer a level of compensation outside the listed range as warranted by the circumstances.

        ]]> At ChristianaCare, we serve as our neighbors as respectful, expert, caring partners in their health. We do this by creating innovative, effective, affordable systems of care that our neighbors value. Supporting this mission, we seek a Vice President, Nursing Quality and Safety. This individual is responsible for providing leadership and strategic direction for quality and patient safety within the Department of Nursing and organization through the design, coordination, implementation and sustainment of initiatives to support a culture of safety and improved outcomes. This role largely leads through influence engaging with other disciplines to drive outcomes. This individual will be a leader, facilitator or member of a number of committees including: Workplace Violence Prevention, Nursing Quality and Safety Council, Event Review Committee, Patient Experience, Zero Harm, Hospital Acquired Pressure Injury Prevention Committee, etc. If this challenging work excites you please apply!

        ChristianaCare benefits:

        ChristianaCare offers an annual incentive bonus and a collegial space where innovation is valued. We offer incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching. Fitness and wellness reimbursement, and phenomenal discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more.

        Our medical/dental/vision benefits are available to our caregivers on their first day of employment! And we offer two different mechanisms toward planning for your retirement, a 403(b) and a defined contribution plan.

        Principal duties and responsibilities:

        • Provides leadership and oversight of resources (staff, operations and capital) to numerous departments within the Department of Nursing Quality & Safety: Nursing Quality and Safety, Wound Ostomy Continence Nursing and the WISH Program.

        • Participates in the development and communication of the Department of Nursing’s strategic vision, plan, objectives, and programs that support quality and safety initiatives.

        • Provides nursing leadership in organizational quality and safety related initiatives. 

        • Serves as an internal consultant to nursing staff, nurse and organizational leaders, and other key stakeholders in promoting a culture of safety and implementing evidence-based practices that improve outcomes.

        • Mentors nurse and organization leaders, clinical nurses and others in identifying and managing improvement opportunities related to patient quality and safety. Advises and mentors clinical nurse leaders and members of the systemwide Nursing Quality & Safety Council and Event Based Nursing Peer Review.

        • Collaborates with organizational leaders and key stakeholders in the design and direction of a quality and safety program(s) and initiatives that are data driven, evidence-based and outcome focused.

        • Oversees and performs departmental budget planning and manages budgets for numerous departments within the Department of Nursing Quality & Safety.

        Education and experience requirements:

        • Master's degree in nursing or related healthcare field required, Doctorate preferred

        • Must possess either BSN or MSN in addition to the above

        • Must possess nursing certification. Seeking Nurse Leader Certification such as NEBC or NEABC required.

        • CPHQ or CPPS preferred, may be acquired within 1 year of starting the role

        • Minimum of five (5) years of progressive involvement in quality/performance improvement activities, with
          demonstrated success at planning, implementing and sustaining performance improvement and quality and
          safety programs.

        • Nursing Leadership experience required

        • Lean Six Sigma training preferred

        • Must possess DE RN license, or appropriate compact license

        ChristianaCare is one of the country’s most multidimensional health systems, centered on improving health outcomes, making high-quality care more accessible and lowering health care costs. Christiana Care includes an extensive network of outpatient services, home health care, medical aid units, three hospitals (1,300 beds), a Level I trauma center and regional centers of excellence in heart and vascular care, cancer care and women’s health. We rank 21st in the nation for hospital admissions.

        ChristianaCare is a not-for-profit teaching health system with our outstanding, data-powered care coordination service and a focus on population health and value-based care, ChristianaCare is crafting the future of health care.

        Working for Christiana Care offers the best of urban and suburban living with multiple housing options, excellent restaurants and entertainment venues, quality schools and a short driving distance to popular Delaware and New Jersey beaches. The location provides easy access to Washington, DC, Philadelphia and New York City – all within a short drive or train ride from Newark, DE.

        ]]> Registered Nurse (RN) – 4N Medical Stepdown

        Night Shift - (Hours: 7p -7a) Weekend & Holidays per department guidelines.

        Wilmington, DE

        ChristianaCare - Wilmington Hospital seeks an experienced Registered Nurse (RN) for our 4N Medical Stepdown Unit. 

        The Unit

        4N is a Medical Stepdown Unit caring for patients with diagnoses affecting all systems: Pneumonia, COPD, HF, Stroke, change in mental status, diabetes, wounds, UTI, Pancreatitis, stroke, and cellulitis.  The patient population includes adolescents, adults, and geriatric patients.  The unit provides staff with various learning experiences in a fast-paced environment. 

        Working on a mixed-acuity unit will allow you to be ACLS certified, learn from critical and emergent patient situations, and use best practice techniques from evidence-based care. We encourage an environment of growth and expanding knowledge, promoting the clinical ladder, openness, and inclusivity. We have nurses who have been on our unit for over 15 years, and welcome anyone who is looking to find a place to grow into an excellent nurse.

        ChristianaCare Offers:

        • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.
        • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions.
        • Generous paid time off with annual rollover and opportunities to cash out.
        • 12-week paid parental leave.
        • Tuition assistance
        • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial mentor, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

        Education & Experience:

        • Delaware RN License or Compact Nursing License.
        • BSN Preferred or commitment to obtaining BSN within 3 years of hire date.
        • RN with 1-2 years of experience in acute care or sub-acute care experience preferred.

Our interdisciplinary team includes physicians, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, nurse navigators, chaplains, and dedicated social workers. Ideal candidates will have a primary interest in doing inpatient consults, follow ups and teaching, with outpatient call coverage
        Interested candidates must have strong interpersonal and communication skills, be an excellent team player and educator, and committed to continuous learning and personal growth. You will help provide clinical leadership and expertise within the palliative care clinical team and identify opportunities to improve the patient and family experience of care and to improve the effectiveness of resource use.

        The schedule will be 4 10hour shifts per week with occasional on-call and weekend coverage as necessary

        Why ChristianaCare?

        ·         Generous Continuing Education Time and Allowance

        ·         Substantial PTO plus 12-Week Fully Paid Parental Leave

        ·         Opportunities for Career Growth

        ·         403(b) and Defined Contribution Plan Retirement Options with Employer Match

        ·         Annual Quality Incentive Bonus


        EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

        •Licensed or eligible for licensure as an Advanced Practice Registered Nurse or Physician Assistant in the state of Delaware

        •Acute Care NP Certification required

        •Advanced Palliative Medicine Certification preferred; or attained within 2 years.
        •Palliative care experience and experience as a provider preferred.
        •BLS required

        ]]> ChristianaCare is currently seeking an Exercise Technician to work at the Center for Rehabilitation at Wilmington Hospital. This department operates 7 days per week with hours from 7:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.

        This position will be required to work two 8-hour shifts, 8:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. or 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. at least two weekdays per week, and two weekend days per month.

        PRIMARY FUNCTION:

        To provide supportive services in the Physical Therapy Department.

        PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

        • Assists the therapist in treatment preparation for group or individual sessions. Sessions include, but are not limited to, hot and cold packs, routine follow-up exercises, and ambulation
        • Maintains the work area in a neat and orderly fashion, safe for patient care. Cleans and prepares equipment for patient use

        • Assists with clerical duties as needed; answering phones, scheduling patient appointments, scheduling patient transportation, scanning, filing, faxing, and communication with referral resources
        • Maintains a current inventory of patient supplies and equipment
        • Assists therapists in medical equipment ordering
        • Monitors patient flow within the department; occasionally assisting with patient transport. May assist with community outings
        • Demonstrates skills and knowledge necessary to provide care appropriate to adolescent, adult, and geriatric patients, including knowledge of growth and development, the ability to obtain and interpret information to identify patient needs, and to provide the care needed

        EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:
        • High School graduate or equivalent
        • Currently enrolled in a pre-PT program through an accredited University is preferred
        • Previous experience working or volunteering in a Physical Therapy setting is preferred

        Any individual hired into this position will be paid $16.50/hour plus any applicable differentials.

        ]]> ChristianaCare's Gastroenterology at Glen Mills is seeking a professional, dependable, detail-oriented Medical Assistant I with strong communication, integrity, and solid technical and clinical skills.

        Gastroenterology at Glen Mills includes a team of expert gastroenterologists who provide comprehensive care for all digestive health needs. Our specialists are highly skilled in diagnosing and treating a wide range of gastrointestinal conditions, ensuring our patients receive the best possible care.

        Gastroenterology | ChristianaCare

        Work Schedule:

        • Mondays through Fridays: 7:45am -4:15pm or 8:00am -4:30pm,

        • Shifts are based on current practice needs.

        • No weekends or holidays.

        • Flexibility to cover at our Havertown practice, strongly preferred.

        Why You’ll Love Working at ChristianaCare

        • Comprehensive, First‑Day Health Coverage:
          Comprehensive first‑day coverage, including medical, dental, vision, and life insurance. Caregivers earning under $50,000 pay $0 in paycheck premiums for medical and prescription coverage. Enjoy low deductibles, plus $0 copays for unlimited primary care (in‑office or virtual) with ChristianaCare providers and unlimited urgent care visits at GoHealth facilities.

        • Generous Paid Time Off
          Enjoy 32 days of PTO each year, including 7 major holidays, with options to roll over or cash out unused time.

        • Tuition Reimbursement for Career Growth
          Advance your education with up to $5,250 per year in tuition reimbursement.

        • Grow Your Family with Confidence
          We’re here to support every step of your journey to parenthood—whatever that looks like for you. From advanced fertility care through Progyny to free maternity education and support, plus up to $5,000 in adoption assistance, our benefits are designed to help you build your family with confidence and care.

        • Paid Parental Leave
          Receive 12 weeks of paid parental leave to support your growing family, after one year of service.

        • Retirement Planning & Financial Well-Being
          Access two retirement plans, including a 403(b) with up to a 50% match and a company-funded contribution of 3%–5% based on service. Plus, take advantage of free financial coaching, counseling, and budgeting support.

        Duties / Responsibilities:
        The Medical Assistant I greets and supports patients virtually and in person, prepares them for visits, performs basic clinical tasks (e.g., testing, injections, EKGs), assists with exams, and maintains exam rooms and equipment. They manage EMR updates, patient communications, referrals, authorizations, and scheduling, while handling clerical duties, troubleshooting video visits, and supporting patient portal access.

        Position Qualifications:

        • High School Graduate or GED required.

        • A graduate of a recognized Medical Assistant Program or hold current Medical Assistant Certification by a national organization accredited by the National Commission for Certifying Agencies (NCCA) or the American National Standards Institute (ANSI).


        Why ChristianaCare
        Do you want to make a meaningful impact in people’s lives? As a Medical Assistant I, you’ll play an essential role in providing high-quality care and supporting better patient outcomes each day. This is more than a job—it’s a chance to be part of a mission-driven team focused on improving healthcare.

        When we lead with love, excellence is inevitable.

        #L1-RT1

        Hourly Pay Range: $19.10 - $27.69

        ]]> Do you want to practice at one of the Top 100 Hospitals in the Nation while delivering meaningful, patient‑centered care?

        At ChristianaCare, our Pharmacy team dispenses more than 8 million individualized patient doses annually, along with 262,000 retail and mail‑order prescriptions, all with a steadfast commitment to safety, quality, and clinical excellence.

        Life is full of choices—and where you work is one of the most important. Join an organization nationally recognized for excellence, innovation, and career growth, where pharmacists are empowered to directly impact patient outcomes.

        ChristianaCare is seeking a Clinical Pharmacist to support our growing, dual‑accredited Specialty Pharmacy Program, embedded within Neurology practices across the health system. This is a hybrid position that floats between multiple neurology practice locations, combining on‑site collaboration with providers and virtual patient care.

        In this role, you will deliver high‑touch, patient‑centric specialty pharmacy services, serving as a trusted medication expert for both patients and neurology providers. You will play a key role in optimizing complex medication therapies, improving access, and enhancing the overall specialty pharmacy experience.

        Key Responsibilities

        • Serve as the primary pharmacy resource embedded within Neurology specialty practices
        • Provide comprehensive education and ongoing monitoring for specialty medications
        • Address medication‑related questions and concerns between provider visits
        • Review and interpret medication orders, assessing:
          • Appropriateness of therapy
          • Efficacy and therapeutic goals
          • Adverse effects and contraindications
          • Drug–drug, drug–food, drug–lab, and drug–disease interactions
        • Monitor and evaluate drug therapy outcomes in accordance with established clinical guidelines
        • Document patient encounters and follow‑ups, adhering to URAC and ACHC accreditation standards
        • Collaborate closely with specialty pharmacists, technicians, access coordinators, and call center teams to ensure seamless prescription processing and patient support
        • Participate in quality improvement initiatives, medication use evaluations, and safety reporting
        • Contribute to the development and revision of pharmacy and health system policies and protocols
        • Provide education and training for pharmacy staff, allied health professionals, and patient groups
        • Precept pharmacy residents and students, and participate in research or scholarly activities as assigned

        Education & Experience Requirements

        • PharmD from an accredited school of pharmacy required
        • Completion of an ASHP‑accredited PGY1 residency or at least 3 years clinical experience required
        • Board Certification (BPS) or Certified Specialty Pharmacist (CSP) preferred
        • Ambulatory care and/or specialty pharmacy experience preferred
        • Current licensure (or eligible to reciprocate) in the State of Delaware
        • Ability to obtain licensure in additional states as required for virtual patient care

        ChristianaCare Offers

        Incredible Work/Life benefits including medical/dental/ vision insurance coverage on your first day of employment, paid PTO, retirement plans, paid parental leave, annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more.

        If you 're inspired to make a difference, we invite you to become a ChristianaCare caregiver!

        ]]> Registered Nurse II - Post Anesthesia/Periop. (PACU Experience REQUIRED)

        Day/Evening shifts (12 hours shifts)

        You may sign up for 84 hours (monthly) commitments in 4 week block.

        Do you thrive on diversity and new experiences in your career?

        Demonstrate your expertise and abilities in a collaborative and encouraging environment where you can work alongside colleagues from various Post-Anesthesia, HVIS/Prep & Holding, Endoscopy, and Surgical Observations Units, all committed to delivering the highest standard of patient care in a diligent organization. ChristianaCare is actively seeking Registered Nurses for our PACU per diem team. We are looking for adaptable individuals ready to work at Christiana Hospital, Christiana Surgery Center, and Wilmington Hospitals as required for operational needs.

        Highlights:

        Our per diem registered nurses serve in various operating rooms at both the Newark and Wilmington campuses. 

        Wilmington Hospital and Christiana Hospital have achieved Magnet Status!

        Enjoy flexible self-scheduling!

        We provide competitive pay rates, including shift and weekend differentials.

        ChristianaCare has been ranked as the No. 5 health system in the nation by Money Magazine in its 2024 hospital rankings. ChristianaCare received an overall grade of A, as well as an A for its dedication to price transparency. Would you like to be part of an organization that continues to strive to be the best?

        What to Expect:

        Pay rates are based upon the amount of hours you commit to every month. You may sign up for 84-hour commitments in 4 weeks blocks. Available shifts will be 12-hour shifts.

        Require to commit to 10 shifts of day shift training the first month to attend New Employee Orientation, Nursing Orientation and training.

        Requirements:

        Commitment to acquiring a BSN within three years of date of hire can be considered with proven experience.

        Qualified applicants are required to have at least three years’ of PACU experience.

        BLS & ACLS required.

        PALS required. (Required to get within 6 months of hire.)

        Christiana Care offers remarkable resources to support our nurses and their careers. As one of the area’s most respected health systems, we know just how significant the nurse’s role is in maintaining the level of quality care we’re known for. Nursing is a centerpiece of the care continuum, valued by physicians and hospital management.

        About Christiana Care:

        Headquartered in Wilmington, Delaware, ChristianaCare is one of the country’s most dynamic health systems, centered on improving health outcomes, making high-quality care more accessible, and lowering healthcare costs. ChristianaCare includes an extensive network of outpatient services, home health care, medical aid units, two hospitals (1,227 beds), a Level I trauma center and a Level III neonatal intensive care unit, a comprehensive stroke center, and regional centers of excellence in heart and vascular care, cancer care and women’s health. ChristianaCare is a not-for-profit guiding health system with more than 260 residents and fellows. We are continually ranked by US News & World Report as the Best Hospital. With our outstanding, data-powered care coordination service and a focus on population health and value-based care, ChristianaCare is crafting the future of healthcare

        ]]> Operating Room Nurse $10K Sign-On-Bonus (for eligible candidate)

        Full-time 80 hours bi-weekly- Day/Evening (8-hour shifts)

        Weekend and Holidays per the guidelines of the department.

        Location: Wilmington

        Are you seeking a Registered Nurse II role with our amazing Wilmington Operating Room team? Christiana Hospital & Wilmington Hospital is the only Level I trauma service on the East Coast corridor between Philadelphia & Baltimore and has been honored repeatedly as "One of America’s Best Hospitals" by U.S. News & World Report and three-times recognized as a Magnet Status Hospital.

        ChristianaCare Benefits

        Incredible Work/Life benefits include an annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial mentor, an on-site gym facility, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and excellent discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

        We also provide tuition assistance for degreed programs, and for some Nursing programs, we offer to pre-pay. Our medical/dental/vision benefits are available to our caregivers on their first day of employment! And we offer two different mechanisms for planning for your retirement, a 403(b) and a defined contribution plan.

        Wilmington Hospital Operating Room team environment is a home away from home. A team that cares about quality and works together in unison and harmony to create a superb outcome. Would you like to be part of this?

        We are currently recruiting for a full-time Registered Nurse to work day/evening - 80 hours bi-weekly. This position will have day rotation to evening shifts, and on-call obligations. Weekend and Holidays per the guidelines of the department.

        The selected RN will be an upbeat Operating Room Registered Nurse who wants to be a part of a growing operating room team passionate about providing excellent clinical outcomes through caring service to our patients and their families.

        Requirements:

        • BSN preferred.
        • Commitment to obtaining within three years of date of hire can be considered with relevant experience.
        • Current RN licensure required
        • At least 2 years of Operating Room Experience is required.
        • Knowledge of multiple specialties preferred.
        • knowledge of ophthalmology and robotics specialties preferred
        • CNOR certification a plus.

        The successful candidate may be eligible for a $10,000 sign-on bonus to be paid over a period of 12 months

        Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

        This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

        ]]> Do you want to work at one of the Top 100 Hospitals in the nation? We are guided by our values of Love and Excellence and are passionate about delivering health, not just health care. Come join us at ChristianaCare!

        ChristianaCare is looking for a full-time operating Room Assistant II to manage and distribute disposable supplies to support the Operating Rooms and ancillary locations.

        Duties and Responsibilities:

        • Fills Case bins / carts with product delivered
        • Places orders using RFID system and Workday handheld
        • Verifies stock levels using RFID system and clean rooms audits
        • Confirms stocking levels of feeding tube supplies for upcoming cases
        • Uses applicable reports to evaluate causes of material when they occur
        • Responds to all emails and Voceras sent by Clinical Staff in department they are assigned
        • Restocks and rotates, stock and non-stock supplies, while checking for damaged and expired products
        • Notifies the inventory clerk or inventory coordinator of any supply shortages or usage increases
        • Inventories and replenishes assigned stock and non-stock par locations in IR Lab, Soft goods, O.R., and ancillary locations
        • Answers incoming phone calls in a courteous manner and provides assistance to O.R. staff
        • Performs assigned work safely, adhering to established departmental safety rules and practices. Reports any unsafe activity, hazards, conditions or violations to supervisor or administrator in a timely manner
        • Visually checks products to verify information and condition of product. Information includes: catalog numbers, expiration dates, sterility, and description.

        Hours: Day Shift Monday - Friday 7a to 3:30p

        Education and Experience Requirements:

        • High School Diploma
        • Minimum three years of relevant work experience in Materials Management and/or Supply Chain.
        • Equivalent amount of applicable Clinical experience will be considered, though ideal candidate will have some Materials Management experience

        Knowledge, Skill, and Ability Requirements

        • Ability to serve customers and maintain a high level of satisfaction
        • Proficient in the use of Microsoft Office products including Excel
        • Skill in oral and written communications like emails and Vocera
        • Ability to do basic numerical calculations
        • Ability to work with little supervision
        • Ability to communicate effectively in situations with a wide range of stress level.
        • Ability to operate all equipment and software used in Surgical Materials.

        Working Conditions:

        • Fast-paced, active, and customer orientated environment. Possible exposure to radiation, anesthesia agents, and other hazardous gases and chemicals.

        Physical Demands:

        • Standing for long periods of time
        • Lifting, carrying, pushing, pulling, bending, and walking
        • Frequent lifting up to 35 lbs.
        • Must be able to push a cart with a maximum of 250 lbs. of stock to the O.R. area.
        • Must be able to visually inspect small packages and verify small text including but not limited to expiration information which may be translucent.

        ChristianaCare Offers:

        • Collaborative and team focused environment
        • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.
        • 403(b)
        • Generous paid time off
        • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

        We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

        This position has a starting rate of $18.75 per hour, regardless of prior experience. After six months of continuous employment, the hourly rate will automatically increase to $21.87 per hour. After one year of continuous employment, the hourly rate will automatically increase to $22.91.

        HIGHLIGHTS

        We are currently hiring for a RN to work 36 hours per week on the Night shift for our Surgical Specialty unit. Typical shift is 7p -7a with Weekends and Holidays, per the guidelines of the department.

        The Surgical Specialty Unit utilizes the nursing process to deliver safe, context-dependent patient and family-centered care in accordance with the American Nurses Association Scope and Standards of Practice, which explicitly delineates those duties that all registered nurses, regardless of role, position, population, or specialty, are expected to perform competently. The Direct-Care Registered Nurse is individually responsible and accountable for maintaining competency as evidenced by integration of nursing knowledge, skills, abilities and judgment. Failure to demonstrate competent nursing practice may result in dismissal. 


        ESSENTIAL JOB RESPONSIBILITIES:
        -  Collects comprehensive data pertinent to the patient’s health and/or the situation. (Assessment)

        -  Analyzes the assessment data to determine the nursing diagnoses or the relevant issues. (Diagnosis)

        -  Identifies expected outcomes for a plan individualized to the patient or the situation. (Outcomes Identification)

        -  Develops a plan that prescribes strategies and alternatives to attain the expected outcomes. (Planning)

        -  Implements the identified plan of care and coordinates care delivery. (Implementation) o Safely administers medication and other treatments as ordered by providers.

        -  Provides or delegates nursing interventions in response to the patient’s medical and nursing diagnoses.

        -  Demonstrates sensitivity to the patient and family’s emotional, spiritual and cultural needs.

        -  Employs strategies to promote health and ensure a safe environment. (Health Teaching and Health Promotion)

        -  Evaluates progress toward the attainment of outcomes and revises the plan of care accordingly. (Evaluation)

        -  Practices ethically.

        -  Attains knowledge and competence that reflects current nursing practice.

        -  Integrates evidence and research findings into practice.

        -  Contributes to quality nursing practice

        -  Communicates effectively in all areas of practice.

        -  Demonstrates leadership in the professional practice setting and the profession.

        -  Collaborates with patients, families, and others in the conduct of nursing practice.

        -  Evaluates her or his own nursing practice in relation to professional practice standards and guidelines, relevant statues, rules and regulations.

        -  Utilizes appropriate resources to plan and provide nursing services that are safe, effective, and financially responsible.

        -  Practices in an environmentally safe and healthy manner.


        REQUIREMENTS:
        -  BSN preferred

        -  Commitment to acquiring BSN within three years of date of hire can be considered with relevant experience.

        -  At least one year med/surg experience as a RN.

        -  BLS required

        Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

        This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

        Ready to Elevate Your OR Nursing Career? Join a Nationally Recognized Team at ChristianaCare!

        Are you a skilled and passionate Operating Room RN looking for your next big opportunity? Christiana Hospital and Wilmington Hospital are proud to offer an exceptional OR RN II position in our state-of-the-art Operating Room.

        As the only Level I Trauma Center between Philadelphia and Baltimore, ChristianaCare is a leader in advanced surgical care. We've been repeatedly named “One of America’s Best Hospitals by U.S. News & World Report and are proud to hold Magnet® Status—a testament to our commitment to nursing excellence.

        Why Join ChristianaCare?

        We don’t just care for patients—we care for our caregivers too. Our benefits are designed to support your well-being, growth, and lifestyle:

        • Day-One Medical/Dental/Vision Coverage

        • Tuition Assistance & Pre-Paid Nursing Programs

        • 403(b) Retirement Plan + Defined Contribution Plan

        • Annual Care.com Membership & Backup Dependent Care

        • Fitness & Wellness Reimbursement

        • Exclusive Discounts on hotels, theme parks, entertainment, and more

        • Financial Mentorship & Retirement Planning Services

        About the Role

        We’re seeking a full-time, experienced Registered Nurse to join our dynamic Operating Room team at Christiana Hospital in Newark, DE.

        You’ll be part of a high-performing, collaborative team that thrives on delivering exceptional outcomes and compassionate care. If you're an energetic OR RN who’s ready to make a meaningful impact in a fast-paced, cutting-edge environment—this is the place for you.

        Come be a part of something extraordinary. Apply today and take your career to the next level with ChristianaCare!

        REQUIREMENTS:

        BSN required or dedication to acquiring within three years of the date of hire

        Current RN licensure required

        Must have Operating Room Registered Nurse Experience.

        CNOR certification a plus. 

        The successful candidate may be eligible for a $10,000 sign-on bonus to be paid over a period of 12 months

        Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

        This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.
