        ]]> Registered Nurse (RN) - Acute Care Rehabilitation

        PT Day Shift - Hours: 7p-7a Weekends & holidays per staffing guidelines.

        Wilmington, DE

        Christiana Care Wilmington Hospital is looking for an experienced Registered Nurse (RN) for Acute Care Rehabilitation.

        ChristianaCare Rehabilitation Services at Wilmington Hospital helps our patients reclaim their independence and activity level, putting them back on the road to their best health. We are an accredited Comprehensive Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility with specialty accreditation in stroke, brain injury, and limb loss by the Commission on Accreditation of Rehabilitation Facilities (CARF).

        Our team of specially trained healthcare professionals at the ChristianaCare Center for Rehabilitation at Wilmington Hospital (CRWH) is dedicated to rehabilitation to help patients regain or improve the skills needed for daily life. Patients receive nursing care, the medical staff manages medical problems, and rehabilitation is provided in our state-of-the-art center. We are internationally recognized and accredited as a Comprehensive Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility with specialty accreditations in stroke, brain injury, and amputation by the Commission on Accreditation of Rehabilitation Facilities (CARF).

        ChristianaCare Offers:

        • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.
        • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions.
        • Generous paid time off with annual rollover and opportunities to cash out.
        • 12-week paid parental leave.
        • Tuition assistance
        • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to Care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial mentor, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets, and much more!

        Education & Experience Requirements:

        • Delaware RN License or Compact Nursing License required.
        • Associate Degree in Nursing (ADN) required. Applicants must commit to obtaining a BSN within three years of the hire date.  
        • Minimum of one year of RN experience required.  
        • Preferred Experience: 1-2 years in Rehabilitation, Med/Surg, or Stepdown.
        • Basic Life Support (BLS) certification required.

        Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

        This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

        FT Night Shift - (Hours: 7p -7:30a with 20% Night Shift Differential Pay!)

        Weekend & Holidays per department guidelines.

        Christiana Care - Wilmington Hospital in DE is looking to hire an experienced Registered Nurse (RN) for our 4W Medical Surgical Unit.

        The Unit

        4West Surgical is a 50-bed medical-surgical inpatient unit located on the fourth floor of the Wilmington Hospital campus, which provides scheduled and emergent care/procedures on a 24-hour basis. The area is designed to accommodate up to 50 patients who need medical and post-operative surgical care. The Registered Nurses in this 4W Surgical Unit care for various surgical patients, including orthopedic, plastics, and more.

        Click here to learn more about ChristianaCare - Medical Surgical Unit - 4W

        ChristianaCare Offers:

        • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.
        • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions.
        • Generous paid time off with annual rollover and opportunities to cash out.
        • 12-week paid parental leave.
        • Tuition assistance
        • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial mentor, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

        Education & Experience Requirements:

        • RN licensure or eligibility for licensure in the state of Delaware.
        • BSN required or commitment to obtaining a BSN within three years of hire date.
        • One year of nursing experience in an acute care setting is required.
        • BLS Certification is required.

          Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

          This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

            ]]> Do you want to work at one of the top 100 hospitals in the nation?

            Our amazing Pharmacy Team dispenses approximately 8,000,000 individualized patient doses per year, as well as 252,000 retail/mail order prescriptions. We provide medications that are safe and efficacious.

            Life is full of choices, and whom you work for is one of life's most important decisions. ChristianaCare has earned national recognition for excellence. Choose an organization that provides career advancement and the opportunity to impact health.

            Come and join us in the ChristianaCare Pharmacy - Dedicated to getting the right drug to the right patient at the right time!

            ChristianaCare is recruiting for a Pharmacy Technician to work full time, evening/midnight shift rotation. Every other weekend required; holidays as required.

            Principal Duties and Responsibilities:

            • Assist the pharmacist in providing timely, efficient, cost effective, and appropriate pharmaceutical care to the patient
            • Refill patient medication bins through robot cart fill and manual picks; update carts by including new orders, removing discontinued medications, and transferring medications. Update labeling of bins on admission, transfer, and discharge of patients
            • Deliver and exchange 24-hour cart fill to nursing units at scheduled times. Check medication storage areas for discontinued and discharged patient medications; ensure that returned medications are placed back into stock
            • Reorder medications in short supply and restock the picking station and other areas of the pharmacy
            • Send prepared medications via pneumatic tube system. Any medication that cannot be tubed is delivered to the appropriate location on the nursing unit
            • Manage automated dispensing cabinet inventory, including accurate selection and loading of medications
            • Ensure that medications are stored properly and that only in date medications are stored. Remove discontinued medication
            • Receive and check incoming supplier order(s)
            • Prepare IV medications using aseptic techniques and in following processes in full compliance with USP 795, 797, and 800 federal regulations
            • Use appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and closed system transfer devices (as needed) for the preparation of chemotherapy and other hazardous medications

            Education and Experience Requirements:

            • High School Diploma required
            • Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB) certification required unless enrolled in a PharmD program.
            • Applicants with Pharmacy Technician experience are encouraged to apply and will be required to obtain PTCB certification within 1 year of employment.
            • Applicants enrolled or graduate of a 4-year bachelor program in the following health science majors are encourage to apply and will be required PTCB certification within 1 year of employment: Applied Molecular Biology and Biotechnology Biology, Chemistry, Exercise Science, Health Behavior Science, Human Physiology, Medical Diagnostics, Medical Diagnostics Pre-PA, Medical Laboratory Science, Nutrition, Nutrition and Dietetics,  Nutrition and Medical Sciences, Nursing,  Occupational Therapy,  Physical Therapy,  Physician Assistant, Sports Health, or other health related degrees
            • Hospital pharmacy experience preferred
            • Computer experience preferred (e.g., window-based programs)
            • Basic typing skills

            Physical demands:

            Standing and walking up to 8 miles a day with intermittent sitting. Occasional extended periods at computer terminal. Required movement throughout ChristianaCare. Requires manual dexterity, the ability to push or pull a fully weighted cart of 250 lbs. which is no more than 15 lbs. and lift up to 50 lbs. as essential components of the position.

            ChristianaCare offers:

            Incredible Work/Life benefits including medical/dental/ vision insurance coverage on your first day of employment, paid PTO, retirement plans, twelve weeks of paid parental leave, annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

            If you 're inspired to make a difference, we invite you to become a ChristianaCare caregiver!

            Hourly Pay Range: $19.84 - $29.76

            This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

              ]]> ChristianaCare Hospital Dentistry Clinic is looking for a Dental Assistant II.
               

              ChristianaCare Hospital Dentistry Clinic is located in Wilmington, Delaware. For more than 60 years, the Department of Oral Maxillofacial Surgery & Hospital Dentistry has provided high‑quality, low‑cost dental care to uninsured and underinsured members of the community. Services are delivered by dental residents who have completed dental school and are pursuing an additional year of hospital-based training. Care is closely supervised by dedicated volunteer community dentists and experienced faculty members.
              Dentistry Services | ChristianaCare


              The Dental Assistant II supports dental and oral surgery care for patients of all ages under the supervision of the Dental Supervisor and Dentist, performing clinical, radiographic, laboratory, and administrative duties to ensure efficient, high-quality care.


              Work Schedule:

              • Monday through Friday from 7:30am to 4pm (flexibility is a must)

              Why You’ll Love Working at ChristianaCare

              • Comprehensive, First‑Day Health Coverage:
                Comprehensive first‑day coverage, including medical, dental, vision, and life insurance. Caregivers earning under $50,000 pay $0 in paycheck premiums for medical and prescription coverage. Enjoy low deductibles, plus $0 copays for unlimited primary care (in‑office or virtual) with ChristianaCare providers and unlimited urgent care visits at GoHealth facilities.
              • Generous Paid Time Off
                Enjoy 32 days of PTO each year, including 7 major holidays, with options to roll over or cash out unused time.
              • Tuition Reimbursement for Career Growth
                Advance your education with up to $5,250 per year in tuition reimbursement.
              • Grow Your Family with Confidence
                We’re here to support every step of your journey to parenthood—whatever that looks like for you. From advanced fertility care through Progyny to free maternity education and support, plus up to $5,000 in adoption assistance, our benefits are designed to help you build your family with confidence and care.
              • Paid Parental Leave
                Receive 12 weeks of paid parental leave to support your growing family, after one year of service.
              • Retirement Planning & Financial Well-Being
                Access two retirement plans, including a 403(b) with up to a 50% match and a company-funded contribution of 3%–5% based on service. Plus, take advantage of free financial coaching, counseling, and budgeting support.

              Duties / Responsibilities:

              The Dental Assistant II supports dental and oral surgery clinics by preparing patients, sterilizing instruments, assisting chairside, and reinforcing post-procedure care while maintaining strict infection control standards. They also provide lab and radiology support, including impressions and dental imaging, and handle clerical tasks such as scheduling, records management, and billing, while delivering strong communication and customer service.

              Position Qualifications:

              • A graduate of accredited Dental Assistant program.
              • Dental Radiology Technicians certified by the Delaware Office of Radiation Control.
              • One-year previous work-related experience.
              • Current BLS certification

              What Makes a Successful Dental Assistant at ChristianaCare:

              A successful Dental Assistant has strong knowledge of dental techniques, instruments, materials, and radiologic procedures, along with proficiency in sterilization and infection control. They demonstrate excellent manual dexterity, skill in four-handed dentistry, oral surgery support, impressions, and dental X-rays, including assisting with sedation or anesthesia. Strong communication, patient support, confidentiality, and safe equipment use are essential.

              When we lead with love, excellence is inevitable.
              #LI-EH1

              ]]> Do you want to work at one of the top 100 hospitals in the nation?

              ChristianaCare is currently recruiting for a Clinical Staff Pharmacist to join our team.

              Life is full of choices, and ChristianaCare recognizes whom you work for is an important decision. Choose an organization that values pharmacy services and provides the opportunity to impact the health of our community adhering to our Core Values and Behaviors guided by Excellence and Love.

              This position is full-time 7 on / 7 off Midnight Shift rotation schedule.  Hours are 9 pm to 8 am Monday through Friday and 9 pm to 9 am on Saturday and Sunday. Holidays and weekends that fall during the week of midnights will be assigned holiday(s) and weekends.

              Why ChristianaCare?

              • At the Newark Campus, operations run through a network of inpatient pharmacists, including a central pharmacy and satellite pharmacies that focus on critical care, emergency medicine, heart failure, investigational drugs, neonates, oncology, pediatrics, perioperative services, sterile and non-sterile compounding, transplant, and trauma.
              • Clinical services include robust acute and ambulatory care services. In the acute care setting, the department employs a decentralized model of practice, placing pharmacists directly into patient care units as part of a multidisciplinary team.
              • Generous PTO plan and 12-Week Fully Paid Parental Leave
              • 403(b) with competitive employer match and Defined Contribution Retirement Plan
              • Not-for-profit organization eligible with the Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF) program
              • Pharmacy Board Certification reimbursement

              As an Inpatient Pharmacist you will:

              • Review and interpret provider orders and dispense medication
              • Evaluate drug efficacy and review for contraindications/precautions
              • Clarify and resolve questioned orders with providers prior to dispensing initial doses.
              • Manage specific medication therapies per approved policies (e.g., warfarin, aminoglycosides, vancomycin, IV to PO conversions, renal dose adjustments, indication-directed dose adjustments, selection of surgical prophylaxis medications)
              • Provide drug information to allied healthcare professionals.
              • Coordinate and supervise work-place activities
              • Assume responsibility of the satellite pharmacies including the sterile product processing in the IV Admixture Service lab and OR pharmacy

              EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

              • Bachelor of Science degree in Pharmacy or PharmD degree from an accredited College of Pharmacy required
              • At least one year of experience working as a pharmacist in an acute care hospital, or;
              • Two years of previous experience working as a Pharmacy Intern, or Pharmacy Technician in an acute care hospital, or
              • Two years of experience as a Pharmacist in an in-patient hospital other than acute care, a long term care setting, or an out-patient Infusion Center
              • Current licensure (or eligible to reciprocate) in the State of Delaware

              About ChristianaCare:

              ChristianaCare is one of the country's most dynamic health systems, centered on improving health outcomes, making high-quality care more accessible and lowering health care costs. ChristianaCare includes an extensive network of outpatient services, home health care, hospital care at home, medical aid units, three hospitals (1,300 beds). We provide a Level I trauma center, Level III neonatal intensive care unit, a comprehensive stroke center and regional centers of excellence in heart and vascular care, cancer care and women's health. We rank 21st in the nation for hospital admissions and 6th for admission of stroke patients. ChristianaCare is a not-for-profit teaching health system with our outstanding, data-powered care coordination service and a focus on population health and value-based care. ChristianaCare is shaping the future of health care!

              If you 're inspired to make a difference, we invite you to become a ChristianaCare caregiver!

The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

              ]]> Do you want to work at one of the top 100 hospitals in the nation?

              Life is full of choices—and where you work is one of the most important.

              At ChristianaCare, we’ve earned national recognition for excellence. Choose an organization that offers career advancement and the opportunity to make a real impact on health.

              Join the ChristianaCare Pharmacy Team—dedicated to getting the right drug to the right patient at the right time!

              In this role, you will serve as a key pharmacy resource within the infusion suites at the Helen F. Graham Cancer Center, ensuring the safe, effective, and efficient use of medications in an outpatient infusion environment. The ideal candidate will provide expert clinical oversight by confirming medication appropriateness, verifying accurate dosing, and ensuring all required lab values, physical assessments, and line access meet established standards. Through this proactive clinical evaluation, you will help optimize patient outcomes while minimizing medication waste.

              Principal Duties and Responsibilities:

              Patient Care

              • Review medication orders—preferably prior to insurance authorization—to ensure alignment with clinical guidelines and individual patient needs.
              • Verify laboratory results, patient status, and appropriate venous access before releasing orders for preparation, ensuring all parameters support safe and effective administration.
              • Monitor therapy and recommend dosage adjustments based on patient response and/or laboratory findings.
              • Collaborate with pharmacists and pharmacy technicians within the HFGCC Pharmacy to support timely medication preparation and delivery to infusion suites.
              • Evaluate medication therapies in accordance with organizational policies and best practices.
              • Participate in identifying and managing adverse drug reactions.
              • Provide individualized patient education as requested or based on assessed needs.
              • Support additional infusion pharmacy clinical operations as needed.
              • May require travel to other ChristianaCare locations

              Quality Control and Efficiency

              • Develop and implement strategies to reduce medication waste and enhance cost‑effective care delivery.
              • Participate in quality improvement initiatives that strengthen patient safety and care quality.
              • Review and update infusion center medication protocols to reflect current clinical guidelines and evidence‑based practices.
              • Assist with interdepartmental projects, including evaluation and optimization of electronic medical record workflows.
              • Ensure proper handling and storage of medications in compliance with federal and state regulations and ChristianaCare policies.

              Documentation and Compliance

              • Maintain accurate and comprehensive documentation of patient interactions, clinical recommendations, and interventions within the electronic medical record.
              • Ensure adherence to all regulatory and accreditation requirements, including those set forth by The Joint Commission, Delaware and Maryland State Boards of Pharmacy, the DEA, and other governing bodies.

              Education and Experience Requirements:

              • PharmD degree from an accredited college or school of pharmacy required
              • Current licensure (or eligible to reciprocate) in the State of Delaware required
              • ASHP-accredited PGY1 Pharmacy Practice Residency OR 2 years of experience in an infusion or health-system setting required.
              • Infusion center experience preferred
              • Board of Pharmacy Specialties certification preferred
              • May require pharmacist licensure in additional states

              ChristianaCare Offers:

              Incredible Work/Life benefits including medical/dental/ vision insurance coverage on your first day of employment, paid PTO, retirement plans, twelve weeks of paid parental leave, annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more.

              ]]> Registered Nurse - Surgical/Trauma ICU  

              ChristianaCare's Christiana and Wilmington hospitals have received their third Magnet® designation for nursing excellence from the American Nurses Credentialing Center, the pre-eminent recognition for hospital systems in the United States! Only 8 percent of the nation’s hospitals hold the prestigious Magnet designation. ChristianaCare was first in Delaware to achieve Magnet status in 2010, and is first in the state to attain re-designation, demonstrating sustained excellence in nursing practice and adherence to national standards! 
              We operate the only Level I Trauma Center between Philadelphia and Baltimore.

              The Surgical Critical Care Complex (SCCC) is a 22 bed Surgical / Trauma Intensive Care Unit. We have a cohesive Nursing Team who serves patients with a multidisciplinary approach. The Nurses on this unit work with neurosurgical, general surgical and adult/pediatric trauma patients.

               
               

              Highlights:

               - We are a 4x Magnet Status Hospital!

               - Beacon Award Winner.

               - Growth Opportunities defined by our Clinical Ladder.

               -  We offer a robust benefits package. Our healthcare benefits include medical, dental & eyecare, starting day one!  Enjoy generous paid time off, competitive pay with shift differentials, tuition reimbursement.  Additional benefits include, dependent care assistance, pet insurance, financial coaching, fitness & wellness reimbursements and discounts on multiple services and events.

              - Advance Your Nursing Skills by providing a higher level of care.


               
               

              Requirements:


              • BSN required; or commitment to obtaining within three years of date of hire


              • Must have minimum 1-2 years stepdown

              • Critical Care experience strongly preferred


              • BLS required; ACLS preferred

              Hours: This is a Day/Midnight rotating schedule of 7 a.m. - 7:30 p.m. and 7 p.m. - 7:30 a.m. Weekend and Holiday schedule is per departmental & organizational practices.

              ]]> RN Case Manager - Day Shift

              M-F (Hours: 8a-4:30p with call rotation every 7 weeks) No Holidays or Weekends!

              Newark, DE

              Sign-on Bonus for External Candidates only!

              ChristianaCare Hospital in Newark, DE, is seeking an RN Case Manager with experience in Discharge Planning in an Acute Care Hospital Setting.

              The RN Case Manager will coordinate care and drive patient progression to establish a discharge plan. This includes functioning as an interdisciplinary team member, creating, implementing, and developing all aspects of discharge planning to establish appropriate, timely, and effective transitions throughout the care continuum.

              The Care Management Model: 

              Our Care Management Triad Team Model is a collaboration between the following:

              • RN Case Manager - Manages patient care, drives patient progression, and establishes a discharge plan.
              • Social Worker – Resolves psycho-social barriers and supports discharge needs.
              • Utilization Management – Reviews patient status for appropriateness and anticipated payer coverage.

              CARE MANAGEMENT:

              • Identify patients who have post-acute care, placement, and complex discharge planning needs based on a comprehensive assessment that includes physical, as well as psycho-social factors/needs.
              • Anticipate, initiate, and establish a discharge plan for patients with post-acute care needs, collaborating with the physician, nurse, and other health care providers, the patient, their family/primary caregiver(s), third-party payers, and employer, following established clinical guidelines, standards, and pathways.
              • Review the admission assessment and collaborate with the primary nurse and other health care providers to ensure a multidisciplinary care plan is in place to meet identified patient care needs and desired outcomes.
              • Identify system issues that serve as barriers to care.  Participates in the development and implementation of strategies to remove the obstacles and facilitate patient progression.

              About Us

              ChristianaCare is located in Delaware and is one of the country’s most dynamic healthcare organizations, centered on improving health outcomes, making high-quality care more accessible, and lowering healthcare costs.

              ChristianaCare includes an extensive network of primary care and outpatient services, home health care, urgent care centers, three hospitals (1,336 beds), a freestanding emergency department, a Level I trauma center, and a Level III neonatal intensive care unit, a comprehensive stroke center and regional centers of excellence in heart and vascular care, cancer care and women’s health. It also includes the pioneering Gene Editing Institute.

              Christiana Care Offers

              • Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, Tuition assistance, etc.
              • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions.
              • Generous paid time off with annual rollover and opportunities to cash out.
              • 12-week paid parental leave
              • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets, and much more!

              Education & Requirements

              • An active RN license in DE or a Compact State is required.
              • BSN required.
              • A minimum of three years of experience as an RN in an acute care hospital setting is required.
              • Critical Care RN with a minimum of three years of experience are encouraged to apply.
              • BLS preferred.
              • Case Management Certification (CMC) is required within 18 months of eligibility.

              Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

              This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting.               ]]>

              Do you want to become a Certified Nursing Assistant? PLEASE DO NOT APPLY IF YOU HAVE COMPLETED A NURSING ASSISTANT COURSE OR HAVE ANY PATIENT CARE EXPERIENCE

              New Program dates will start on Monday, July 6th and August 27th, 2026

              Do you desire to help people?

              Do you want to work at one of the Top 100 Hospitals in the nation?

              We are guided by our values of Love and Excellence and are passionate about delivering health, not just health care. Come join us at ChristianaCare!

              ChristianaCare, with Hospitals in Wilmington, DE, Newark, DE and Elkton, MD, is one of the largest health care providers in the Mid-Atlantic Region. Named one of “America’s Best Hospitals by U.S. News & World Report, we have an excess of 1,299 beds between our hospitals and are committed to providing the best patient care in the region. We are proud to that Christiana Hospital, Wilmington Hospital, our Ambulatory Services, and HomeHealth have all received ANCC Magnet Recognition®.

              Check out this important Opportunity:

              Patient Care Technician/Certified Nursing Assistant Residency Program

              The (PCT) patient care technician/(CNA)certified nursing assistant residency program is a unique paid 10-week program in partnership with the Delaware Skills Center, Dawn Career Institute and Christiana Care Health System that integrates classroom and clinical components. Upon successful completion of the 6-week Certified Nursing Assistant Course, the individual will take the Delaware State Test for Nurse Aide Certification. The candidate will then transition to Christiana Care and complete approximately 4 weeks of clinical training.

              • The first 6 weeks of instructor led didactic and clinical with Delaware Skills Center in New Castle, DE or Dawn Career Institute in Newark, DE

              • The following 4 weeks of preceptor led clinical training in an acute care inpatient setting at one of our campuses in Newark or Wilmington

              • NOTE:  If selected for this program – the starting rate of pay will be $16.50 hourly with healthcare benefits during the training and fully paid tuition

              • THIS IS PROGRAM IS CRAFTED FOR INDIVIDUAL'S THAT HAVE A DESIRE TO BECOME A CERTIFIED NURSING ASSISTANT                                                     

              • PLEASE DO NOT APPLY IF YOU HAVE COMPLETED A NURSING ASSISTANT COURSE OR HAVE PATIENT CARE EXPERIENCE

              • This program will start on one of the following date selected for you: Monday, July 6th and August 17th, 2026

              This program requires that you attend Full Time for 6 weeks Monday through Friday between 6:30am – 4:00pm for 6 weeks. Once the residency program is complete, at the end of the 6 weeks, you will be assigned to work in a ChristianaCare unit for one year or 1800 hours per agreement. The working locations will be at Newark, DE, or Wilmington, DE Campus working full-time/flex (36 hours weekly/72 hours bi-weekly) or Part time (24 hours bi-weekly) including every other weekend and rotating holidays (Day/Eve, Evening/Midnight, Day/Midnight and Straight Eve shifts with 8 and 12 hour rotations are being filled with these roles).

              Please attach your updated resume with recommended cover letter to your application for consideration!

              Applications without a resume may not be considered due to the high volume of candidates.

              PRIMARY FUNCTION:
              The Patient Care Technician (PCT) supports activities of daily living under the direction and supervision of the Registered Nurse. The PCT must demonstrate the skills and knowledge necessary to provide care to adolescent, adult, and geriatric patients.  Primary job duties include, but not limited to, performing and accurately recording vital signs, Accu-checks, bathing, grooming, toileting, feeding, and mobilizing patients.  PCT's are an essential part of the nursing care team with an expectation to support the organizations patient safety goals and initiatives.
               
              PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 

              • Demonstrates skills and knowledge necessary to provide care appropriate to patients in various stages of development.

              • Performs assigned nursing duties for the comfort and well-being of patients.

              • Responds to patient crisis situation and initiates interventions within the scope of responsibilities and notifies RN.

              • Performs successful finger sticks on patients, following all standard laboratory safety policies.

              • Performs defined procedures on point of care instrumentation.

              • Provides 1:1 observation for 1-2 patient(s) experiencing a psychiatric crisis, suicidal/homicidal ideation under the order of a physician in Powerchart.

              • Provides 1:1 observation for 1-2 patient(s) at risk for safety based on nursing assessment and order in Powerchart

              • Responds with promptness, empathy and interest to needs of patients.

              • Communicates to patients, employees, and physicians utilizing courteous verbal and written communication skills.

              • Completes mandatory in-service education.

              • Performs assigned work safely, adhering to established departmental safety rules and practices; reports to supervisor, in a timely manner, any unsafe activities, conditions, hazards, or safety violations that may cause injury to oneself, other employees, patients and visitors.

              • Performs other related duties as required.

                
               EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

              • High school graduate or equivalent

              SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS:

              • BCLS Certification required, or Acceptance into a Christiana Care approved Patient Care Technician training program

              SCOPE, PURPOSE, AND FREQUENCY OF CONTACTS:

              • Daily contact with patients, family members, visitors, employees, medical staff, volunteers, and students.

              PHYSICAL DEMANDS:

              • Ability to stand (up to 8 to 12 hours), walk, sit, bend, lift, push, pull, carry, reach, twist, handle, stoop, climb as per unit based Physical Demand Checklist.

              DIRECTION/SUPERVISION RECEIVED:

              • Nurse Manager.  Receives direction from RN

              WORKING CONDITIONS:

              • Frequent exposure to biological elements with occasional exposure to chemical and environmental elements, as per unit-based Physical Demands Checklist.

              KNOWLEDGE, SKILL, AND ABILITY REQUIREMENTS:

              Upon successful completion of the Patient Care Technician Residency Program:

              Knowledge of basic nursing care provided for specific patient population of unit.

              Knowledge of department standards, selected procedures, nursing practice guidelines and procedures.

              Ability to obtain information to identify patient needs and to provide care as needed.

              Ability to work quickly and accurately

              Ability to recognize changes in patient status, take appropriate action and notify RN

              Ability to communicate effectively utilizing verbal and written communication skills.

              Ability to perform basic math skills.

              Ability to use appropriate safety measures for patients, employees and self.

              Ability to follow universal precautions at all times.

              Ability to exercise judgment, tact and diplomacy.

              Ability to respond to patient crisis situations under the direction of the RN, following CCHS protocol

              Ability to demonstrate proficiency in basic computer skills

              Christianacare Offers:

              • Collaborative and team focused environment
              • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.
              • Generous paid time off with annual roll-over and opportunities to cash out
              • 12 week paid parental leave after one year of full-time service
              • Tuition assistance
              • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial mentor, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and phenomenal discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

                Hourly Pay Range: $16.50 - $16.50

                This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                Christiana Care Health System is an equal opportunity employer, firmly committed to prohibiting discrimination, whose staff is reflective of its community, and considers qualified applicants for open positions without regard to race, color, sex, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, genetic information, gender identity or expression, age, veteran status, disability, pregnancy, citizenship status, or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law.

                ]]> Registered Nurse (RN) - Center for Rehabilitation (Acute Care)

                FT Day Shift (Hrs: 7a-7p) Weekends & holidays per department guidelines.

                Wilmington, DE

                ChristianaCare's Center for Rehabilitation at Wilmington Hospital is looking for an experienced Registered Nurse (RN).

                ChristianaCare Rehabilitation Services at Wilmington Hospital helps our patients reclaim their independence and activity level, putting them back on the road to their best health. We are an accredited Comprehensive Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility with specialty accreditation in stroke, brain injury, and limb loss by the Commission on Accreditation of Rehabilitation Facilities (CARF).

                Our team of specially trained healthcare professionals at the ChristianaCare Center for Rehabilitation at Wilmington Hospital (CRWH) is dedicated to rehabilitation to help patients regain or improve the skills needed for daily life. Patients receive nursing care, the medical staff manages medical problems, and rehabilitation is provided in our state-of-the-art center. We are internationally recognized and accredited as a Comprehensive Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility with specialty accreditations in stroke, brain injury, and amputation by the Commission on Accreditation of Rehabilitation Facilities (CARF).

                ChristianaCare Offers:

                • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.

                • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions.

                • Generous paid time off with annual rollover and opportunities to cash out.

                • 12-week paid parental leave.

                • Tuition assistance

                • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to Care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial mentor, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets, and much more!

                Education & Experience Requirements:

                • Delaware RN License or Compact Nursing License required.

                • Associate Degree in Nursing (ADN) required. Applicants must commit to obtaining a BSN within three years of the hire date.  

                • Minimum of one year of RN experience required.  

                • Preferred Experience: 1-2 years in Rehabilitation, Med/Surg, or Stepdown.

                • Basic Life Support (BLS) certification required.

                Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

                This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                ]]> PRIMARY FUNCTION:

                This position will require weekend work, holiday rotation and on-call rotation. The position is responsible for monitoring, troubleshooting and resolving critical infrastructure issues 24 hours per day 7 days a week. These systems and components include but are not limited to:

                • Monitoring network switches, routers, wireless controllers/access points via Extreme Cloud IQ, Aruba, SevOne, Spectrum and Clearpass
                • Monitoring data center security and environmentals via JACE and Genetec
                • Monitoring enterprise storage/backup solutions via EMC and PPDM
                • Monitoring physical server infrastructure via Oneview
                • Monitoring VMWare server infrastructure via Aria Operations, and HP Oneview and Nagios
                • Monitoring of multiple cloud environments via Nagios and Aria Operations
                • Monitoring and maintaining CMDB, incident, problem, change and vulnerability management via ServiceNOW.
                • Monitoring and maintaining vulnerability/patch management via SCCM
                • Monitoring and maintaining SSL certificate issues/renewals via Digicert
                • Monitoring and maintaining monthly infrastructure patch cycles via SCCM
                • Coordinating and executing disaster recovery exercises during planned outages and emergency outages

                PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

                • Monitor and operate control consoles of computer systems, network, facilities, and all attached peripheral equipment. Uses tools that subject matter experts recommend aiding with incident or problem management.
                • Optimizes system utilization by adjusting system resources to meet Service Level Agreements. Intermediate remediation and consult with SME to modify system resources to meet performance or availability goals.
                • Detects, analyzes and resolves routine computer production processing incidents / events. Advanced events, incidents, problems and consulting with engineers/subject matter experts for complicated situations.
                • Detects, troubleshoots, analyzes and resolves system alerts and incidents. Expert troubleshooting skills and able to address system alerts and customer incidents.  Engage/consult with appropriate expert to receive guidance on additional troubleshooting or remediation steps. Create knowledge articles and provide education/turnover to other NOC caregivers.  
                • Coordinates and assists in execution of planned scheduled outages for disaster recovery testing. Acts as a point person for the NOC when performing disaster recovery procedures during unplanned outages.
                • Identifies and pursues opportunities for the continuing professional development enabling increased understanding of the various Christiana Care functions and how new information technology can be applied at Christiana Care. Uses critical infrastructure technology components and key systems to create development plan while engaging engineers/system experts as needed to enhance skills and knowledge.
                • Interacts with team members and executives/management in a friendly, supportive and timely manner. Proactive in communicating trends for awareness and action. Actively participates in major incidents providing valuable troubleshooting and analysis from various monitoring tools.
                • Keeps abreast of all key critical technologies and educates caregivers within the team looking for opportunities for improvement.
                • Learns and applies the internal workings of computer system to the automation tools to effectively manage system resources, operations and processes across multiple platforms. Identify opportunities to create automation to reduce manual intervention for repeat operational work items.
                • Monitors system performance and capacities, making recommendations toward optimizing system productivity. Investigates basic problems, malfunctions, and errors using all available expertise and assistance to determine corrective measures in a timely fashion to maintain a viable production environment. Respond to alarms using all available tools and resources to identify performance and capacity trends to eliminate incidents or problems.
                • Provides basic technical assistance, support, and turnover training to maintain operational readiness. Provide on-site assistance to engineers and application owners as needed.
                • Addresses outdated and incorrect procedures and processes. Use trend information to make recommendations to experts and engineers, while ensuring procedures are updated.

                EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

                • Bachelor's degree in Information Resource Management, Management Information Systems, Computer Science, Information Technology or experience consistent with the Network Operation Analyst job responsibilities. 
                • 3 – 5 years of experience in Information Services.
                • Should be functioning without close supervision within a nine-month period.

                ChristianaCare Offers:

                • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.
                • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions
                • Generous paid time off with annual roll-over and opportunities to cash out
                • 12 week paid parental leave
                • Tuition assistance
                • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

                ]]> ChristianaCare's Primary Care at Jennersville is seeking a professional, dependable, detail-oriented Medical Assistant I with strong communication, integrity, and solid technical and clinical skills.

                Primary Care at Jennersville is located at 900 West Baltimore Pike in West Grove, Pennsylvania. ChristianaCare Primary Care at Jennersville provides comprehensive primary medical care services for both adults and children. The range of services available at this facility encompasses immunizations, physical examinations, preventive medicine, and the treatment of various acute and chronic conditions. This makes it a vital resource for the community's health care needs, addressing a wide spectrum of medical concerns and promoting overall wellness.
                Jennersville | ChristianaCare

                As a Medical Assistant I, you support physicians in virtual and on-site patient care, performing essential clinical and clerical duties to ensure quality care.

                Work Schedule:

                • Monday - Friday: Day shift

                • Rotational schedule shared among all MAs, with shifts starting as early as 6:45 AM (Tuesdays to Fridays) and ending no later than 5:00 PM on assigned days.

                • No weekends or holidays

                Why You’ll Love Working at ChristianaCare

                • Comprehensive, First‑Day Health Coverage:
                  Comprehensive first‑day coverage, including medical, dental, vision, and life insurance. Caregivers earning under $50,000 pay $0 in paycheck premiums for medical and prescription coverage. Enjoy low deductibles, plus $0 copays for unlimited primary care (in‑office or virtual) with ChristianaCare providers and unlimited urgent care visits at GoHealth facilities.

                • Generous Paid Time Off
                  Enjoy 32 days of PTO each year, including 7 major holidays, with options to roll over or cash out unused time.

                • Tuition Reimbursement for Career Growth
                  Advance your education with up to $5,250 per year in tuition reimbursement.

                • Grow Your Family with Confidence
                  We’re here to support every step of your journey to parenthood—whatever that looks like for you. From advanced fertility care through Progyny to free maternity education and support, plus up to $5,000 in adoption assistance, our benefits are designed to help you build your family with confidence and care.

                • Paid Parental Leave
                  Receive 12 weeks of paid parental leave to support your growing family, after one year of service.

                • Retirement Planning & Financial Well-Being
                  Access two retirement plans, including a 403(b) with up to a 50% match and a company-funded contribution of 3%–5% based on service. Plus, take advantage of free financial coaching, counseling, and budgeting support.

                Duties / Responsibilities:
                The Medical Assistant I greets and supports patients virtually and in person, prepares them for visits, performs basic clinical tasks (e.g., testing, injections, EKGs), assists with exams, and maintains exam rooms and equipment. They manage EMR updates, patient communications, referrals, authorizations, and scheduling, while handling clerical duties, troubleshooting video visits, and supporting patient portal access.

                Position Qualifications:

                • High School Graduate or GED required.

                • A graduate of a recognized Medical Assistant Program or hold current Medical Assistant Certification by a national organization accredited by the National Commission for Certifying Agencies (NCCA) or the American National Standards Institute (ANSI).

                Why ChristianaCare
                Do you want to make a meaningful impact in people’s lives? As a Medical Assistant I, you’ll play an essential role in providing high-quality care and supporting better patient outcomes each day. This is more than a job—it’s a chance to be part of a mission-driven team focused on improving healthcare.

                When we lead with love, excellence is inevitable.
                #LI-RT1

                ]]> Do you want to work at one of the Top 100 Hospitals in the nation? We are guided by our values of Love and Excellence and are passionate about delivering health, not just health care. Come join us at ChristianaCare!

                ChristianaCare, with Hospitals in Wilmington and Newark, DE, as well as Elkton, MD, is one of the largest health care providers in the Mid-Atlantic Region. Named one of “America’s Best Hospitals by U.S. News & World Report, we have an excess of 1,100 beds between our hospitals and are committed to providing the best patient care in the region. We are proud to that Christiana Hospital, Wilmington Hospital, our Ambulatory Services, and HomeHealth have all received ANCC Magnet Recognition®.

                ChristianaCare Offers

                • Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.

                • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions.

                • Generous paid time off with annual roll-over and opportunities to cash out.

                • 12-week paid parental leave

                • Tuition assistance

                • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

                ChristianaCare is searching for a full time Clinical Information Analyst to support the ongoing needs of the staff of the Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine and its customers by optimizing the use of the Laboratory information System (LIS) to provide quality patient services. In addition, the analyst will incorporate a strong laboratory technology background while acting as the clinical liaison between the laboratory application side and the technical orientation of the Clinical Information System (CIS) team.

                The successful candidate will have experience as a Medical Laboratory Scientist and have a minimum of three years laboratory testing experience. In addition, the analyst must have the ability to organize, define, and articulate system issues reported by staff and collaborate with IT technical teams to resolve them, Also, have the ability to work closely within laboratory section and IT requirements to meet timely project schedules

                Education Requirements

                • B.S. or equivalent experience in Laboratory Science or Information Technology.

                • Three years of laboratory testing experience.

                • Demonstrated experience supporting a lab information system upgrade (e.g., Data Base Coordinator or Super User).

                • Knowledge of clinical laboratory concepts, techniques, and procedures.

                • Knowledge of laboratory safety and infection control techniques.

                • Knowledge of business operating systems and office applications.

                • Ability to assess situations, consider alternatives, and choose appropriate courses of action.

                • Skill in oral and written communication, and the ability to balance multiple projects simultaneously.

                • Manual dexterity and normal color vision.

                Responsibilities

                • Triage LIS problems presented by laboratory personnel, resolving uncomplicated problems before presentation to LIS Coordinator and/or CIS analysts. Completes all necessary fact finding and compilation of documentation before presenting complex problems to CIS.

                • Participate in set-up and maintenance of foreign systems that interface orders and/or results to the laboratory information system mapping and display of ChristianaCare (CC) and non-CC laboratory data. Have a working knowledge of each of these systems and serve as a training and troubleshooting resource.

                • Have a working knowledge of the concepts related to laboratory medical device interfaces (i.e. instruments).

                • Have a working knowledge of the concepts related to reference laboratory testing including both interfaced (i.e. Mayo, Quest) and non-interfaced testing

                • Perform various audits of the LIS in conjunction with database coordinators to verify ongoing accuracy of data, including billing, test volume, cumulative reports, error correction reports, etc.

                • Have a familiarity with charge capture concepts including billing stage and charge reversals in the LIS and charge processing and display in the patient financial system.

                • Participate in upgrade project teams as the laboratory representative for planning, scheduling, testing, training and communication.

                Hourly Pay Range: $35.39 - $56.63

                This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                ]]> PRIMARY FUNCTION:

                The Manager, Anesthesia is responsible for overseeing the daily operations and activities of the Anesthesia Technologist in accordance with standards established by the American Society of Anesthesia Technologists and Technicians (ASATT). The role requires the ability to organize, coordinate, and supervise all departmental functions, including airway management and ventilation support, management of fluids and blood components, anesthesia-related pharmacology and pathophysiology support, anesthesia equipment preparation, utilization of biotechnology and patient monitoring systems, and supply and inventory management. The manager ensures compliance with ChristianaCare policies, regulatory standards, and unit-level procedures while actively participating in departmental and organizational performance improvement initiatives. Additionally, the Manager plans, organizes, and directs the work of anesthesia technical personnel to support physicians and CRNAs in the safe and effective delivery of anesthesia care and high-quality patient outcomes.

                PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

                • Oversee daily operations of the Anesthesia Technology Department, ensuring efficient clinical and technical support for anesthesia services.
                • Develop and manage staff schedules to maintain appropriate coverage for anesthesia services across all shifts.
                • Ensure departmental operations align with budget targets, operational goals, and cost-effective resource utilization.
                • Lead recruitment, onboarding, retention, and performance management of anesthesia technologists and technicians.
                • Coordinate staff orientation, continuing education, and competency validation, including probationary and annual assessments.
                • Maintain operational readiness of anesthesia equipment, supplies, and the clinical environment.
                • Perform and oversee quality control, testing, troubleshooting, and alarm response for anesthesia machines and related equipment.
                • Collaborate with anesthesia leadership, medical staff, perioperative leaders, and administrative teams to support efficient operations.
                • Monitor compliance with organizational policies, regulatory standards, and internal departmental procedures.
                • Manage caregiver scheduling, daily assignments, and time-off requests to ensure staffing coverage.
                • Conduct operational huddles and coordinate education and training initiatives with the Nursing Professional Development Specialist (NPDS) and education leadership.
                • Support the Department of Anesthesia Chair, site directors, and anesthesia providers to facilitate clinical workflow and service delivery.
                • Develop and monitor departmental operating and capital budgets in collaboration with perioperative business leadership.
                • Oversee procurement, inventory management, and standardization of anesthesia supplies and equipment.
                • Maintain policies and procedures related to anesthesia technician services, equipment preparation, and supply management.
                • Ensure regulatory and accreditation readiness at the unit level.
                • Maintain documentation related to equipment requisition, maintenance, and repair.
                • Lead departmental performance improvement initiatives.
                • Provide operational support to the Operating Room, Heart and Vascular Interventional Services (HVIS), Labor and Delivery, and other procedural areas requiring anesthesia services.

                SCOPE, PURPOSE, AND FREQUENCY OF CONTACTS:

                Frequent contact with nursing staff, patients, families, visitors, medical/dental staff, ancillary staff, students, community agencies, vendors, staff from other hospitals, and public.

                DIRECTION/SUPERVISION OF OTHERS:

                Anesthesia Technician/Technologist

                Anesthesia Lead Technician/Technologist

                DIRECTION/SUPERVISION RECEIVED:

                Reports to Director, Perioperative Services Support Services

                Sr. Vice President Procedural Platforms

                Chair, Department of Anesthesia

                EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

                • Associate’s degree in a healthcare-related field (e.g., Anesthesia Technology, Respiratory Therapy, or Health Sciences); bachelor’s degree preferred.

                • Minimum of 3–5 years of leadership or supervisory experience in a healthcare environment.

                • Experience working in a fast-paced, high-acuity setting such as a Level I trauma center.

                • Proficiency with inventory management systems (e.g., PeopleSoft or similar enterprise systems).

                • Completion of an accredited anesthesia technician training program and certification as a Cer.A.T. or Cer.A.T.T., or eligibility through the Practical Experience Pathway administered by the American Society of Anesthesia Technologists and Technicians (ASATT).preferred or must be completed within one year.

                • Demonstrated leadership or management training in areas such as team development, communication, and conflict resolution.

                • Current Basic Life Support (BLS) certification required.

                • Advanced certifications such as Advanced Cardiovascular Life Support (ACLS) preferred.

                KNOWLEDGE, SKILL, AND ABILITY REQUIREMENTS:

                • Demonstrates knowledge of operating room/anesthesia supplies, equipment and instrumentation.
                • Demonstrates knowledge of appropriate chain of command.
                • Demonstrates knowledge of CCHS Values and Behaviors and departmental policies/procedures.
                • Demonstrates computer skills and the ability to utilize various software applications.
                • Effectively problem solves technical issues effectively.
                • Demonstrates excellent interpersonal skills by effectively dealing with/managing others at a variety of
                • levels with diplomacy and tact.
                • Adapts to a high volume, high stress area.
                • Ability to be flexible and adaptable under constantly changing situations.
                • Makes administrative decisions independently, utilizing sound judgment with established standards.
                • Organizes work while managing multiple tasks.
                • Communicates efficiently and effectively in both written and verbal form.
                • Works independently with established guidelines.
                • Maintains confidentiality of patient medical records, hospital staff, physician and administrative matters.
                • Performs required technical skills as outlined in procedures.
                • Follows standard precautions and uses appropriate safety measures for patients, coworkers and self.

                SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS:

                Completion of an accredited anesthesia technician training program and certification as a Cer.A.T. or Cer.A.T.T., or eligibility through the Practical Experience Pathway administered by the American Society of Anesthesia Technologists and Technicians (ASATT).

                PHYSICAL DEMANDS:

                Ability to stand, walk, sit, bend, lift, carry, reach, twist, handle, stoop, and climb as per unit based physical demands checklist.

                WORKING CONDITIONS:

                Frequent exposure to biological and environmental elements with occasional exposure to chemical elements as per unit-based Physical Demands Checklist.

                Annual Compensation Range $63,107.20 - $100,984.00

                This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                ]]> PRIMARY FUNCTION:

                To assist the surgical team, under the direction of a professional nurse, in transporting patients, managing room turnover, and maintaining cleanliness, supplies, and equipment.

                Per-Diem:

                • Commitment to work either 60 or 84 hours per month
                • Shifts will consist of: 11:30a-8:00p and 3p-11p
                • Weekend Commitment: Every Other Weekend

                PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

                • Demonstrates proficient skills in the PAT role and performs patient care duties that contribute to the efficient, safe operations of the unit which may include but are not limited to: 
                  • Performing appropriate skin prep procedures adhering to Procedure Area policy/procedure  
                  • Assisting patient care team in the transfer of patient to and from bed/stretcher 
                  • Assisting patient care team in positioning patient on Procedure Area table or holding patient extremity for skin prep 
                  • Transporting of the patient to and from Procedure Area or department 
                • Cross-trained into either the perianesthesia tech role (to support PACU), the clerk role (to support the OR or PACU desk), or SPD certification (scope/ instrument processing support)
                • Provides necessary equipment/supplies for the patient care team which may include but not limited to:      
                  • Maintaining and Restocking supplies 
                  • Procedure Area room set up-- provides appropriate equipment/tables and obtains case cart or instrument sets needed for procedure. 
                  • Performs between case cleaning procedures 
                    • Cleaning entire Procedure Area room using infection control standards
                    • Cleaning specialty equipment/instrumentation/ carts 
                    • Returning used soft goods and instrument sets appropriately. 
                    • Appropriately handling contaminated instrument trays, carts, specimens, and body fluids. 
                • Performs general area cleaning which may include but not limited to: 
                  • Daily dusting, sweeping, mopping, care and maintenance to sub sterile areas, breakrooms, unsterile hallways, and other areas as needed. 
                • Troubleshoots minor equipment problems and initiates service calls for broken equipment as needed. 
                • Demonstrates knowledge, skills, and abilities to safely provide age-appropriate care to the patients served, under the direction of the RN. 
                • Responds to crisis situations and performs essential tasks under the direction of the RN. 
                • Professional telephone etiquette 
                • Functions as an effective team member, supporting the concept of team training.
                • Actively participates in unit activities
                • Communicates effectively with patients, families, employees, and physicians, both verbally and in writing.
                • Acts as a preceptor for new employees in specific areas of expertise to improve clinical practice
                • Reports to supervisor, in a timely manner, any unsafe activities, conditions, hazards, or safety violations that may cause injury to oneself, other employees, patients and visitors.
                • Performs other related duties as required.

                SCOPE, PURPOSE, AND FREQUENCY OF CONTACTS:

                Daily contact with patients, employees, physicians, and students.  Contact at various time intervals with families, visitors, community agencies, companies as well as public.

                DIRECTION/SUPERVISION OF OTHERS:

                PAT I

                DIRECTION/SUPERVISION RECEIVED:

                Reports to Perioperative Supervisor.  Additional supervision by Nurse Manager, ANMs, PAT IV

                Direction from local department RN/ST caregivers

                MINIMUM EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

                High school diploma or equivalent. 

                A minimum of two years’ full-time clinical practice is required, Perioperative experience preferred.

                KNOWLEDGE, SKILL, AND ABILITY REQUIREMENTS:

                Knowledge, skills, and abilities related to scrub responsibilities for the care of the neonate, pediatric,

                adolescent, adult, and geriatric patient in the OR.

                Knowledge of PCT responsibilities related to emergency procedures (Code blue, Code red, Code delta, heart alert, stroke alert)

                Completes mandatory education

                Basic knowledge of medical terminology

                Maintains professional appearance wearing Christiana Care issued scrubs when appropriate

                Ability to act independently within established procedures and protocols.

                Ability to perform specific unit tasks as assigned by the RN.

                Ability to exercise judgement, tact and diplomacy.

                Ability to make decisions utilizing sound judgement.

                Ability to maintain patient confidentiality.

                Ability to utilize computer and keyboard.

                SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS:

                BCLS certification

                PHYSICAL DEMANDS:

                Ability to stand, walk, sit, bend, lift, push, pull, carry, reach, twist, handle, stoop, climb as per unit-based Physical Demands Checklist.

                WORKING CONDITIONS:

                Frequent exposure to biological, chemical and environmental elements, as per unit-based Physical Demands Checklist.

                Hourly Pay Range: $19.84 - $29.76

                This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                ]]> The ChristianaCare Microbiology Laboratory located on the Newark campus, is currently recruiting for a Medical Laboratory Scientist/Technician.

                This position is full time, Morning shift, in the Newark Microbiology Lab. The work hours are Monday through Friday, 11:00pm - 7:30am. and every third weekend and 2-3 holidays per year. 

                Responsibilities:

                • Accurately performs all test procedures as listed in the procedure manual.

                • Performs and records all scheduled instrument preventative maintenance procedures.

                • Follows established quality control procedures, documenting corrective action if necessary.

                • Adheres to all turnaround times, documents calls as necessary, verifies critical values and specimen identification.

                • Verifies completion of all stat results and all routine results in a timely manner.

                • Records workload and other statistics into computer system as necessary.

                • Performs assigned work safely adhering to established department safety rules and practices.

                • Performs other related duties as instructed.

                Requirements:

                • BS in Medical Technology or Medical Laboratory Science
                  BS in a chemical, physical, biological science

                • AAS in Medical Technology or Medical Laboratory Science

                • ASCP eligible or equivalent

                • Knowledge of clinical laboratory techniques and procedures

                • Knowledge of infection control techniques and procedures

                • Ability to coordinate simultaneous tests and procedures with accuracy

                • Skill in the use of automated laboratory equipment

                When you become an employee at ChristianaCare, you are joining a robust healthcare organization that truly cares about their patients and their caregivers. For the second consecutive year, Forbes magazine has ranked ChristianaCare as one of the best large health systems to work for in the United States! Guided by excellence and love, our Caregivers enjoy a multitude of employee benefits that include:

                • Full Medical, Dental, Vision and other insurance benefits

                • 403 (b) with an employer match

                • Diverse and Inclusive culture

                • Generous paid time off with annual roll-over and opportunities to cash out

                • 12 week paid parental leave

                About Christiana Care:

                Headquartered in Wilmington, Delaware, ChristianaCare is one of the country’s most dynamic health systems, centered on improving health outcomes, making high-quality care more accessible and lowering health care costs. ChristianaCare includes an extensive network of outpatient services, home health care, medical aid units, two hospitals (1,227 beds), a Level I trauma center and a Level III neonatal intensive care unit, a comprehensive stroke center and regional centers of excellence in heart and vascular care, cancer care and women’s health. ChristianaCare is a not-for-profit teaching health system with more than 260 residents and fellows. We are continually ranked by US News & World Report as a Best Hospital. With our unique, data-powered care coordination service and a focus on population health and value-based care, ChristianaCare is shaping the future of health care.

                Hourly Pay Range: $29.22 - $43.83

                This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.
