                    ]]> Assistant Manager/Assistant Nurse Manager - HVIS Interventional Labs

                    Full Time - 80 hours/pay

                    Newark, DE

                    ChristianaCare is looking for an Assistant Manager/Assistant Nurse Manager to assist the Nurse Manager in overseeing daily operations and ensuring efficient workflow within the Heart and Vascular Intervention Service (HVIS), which includes the Cardiac Cath Lab, Structural Heart Lab, Electrophysiology Lab, Vascular Interventional Radiology, and Endovascular Neurosurgery. Share 24-hour responsibility and accountability for all aspects of daily operational activities with the Nurse Manager.

                    ​

                    Assistant Manager Education & Requirements:

                    • Graduate from an accredited Radiologic Technology program or Cardiovascular Technology program required

                    • Bachelor’s degree required with 5 years of experience in Interventional Labs: Cardiac, Electrophysiology, Endovascular and/ or Neuro

                    • Or Associate’s degree with six years of experience in Interventional Labs: Cardiac, Electrophysiology, Endovascular and/ or Neuro.  Must obtain Bachelor’s degree within three years of hire or transfer date.

                    • Registered Technologist certified through American Registry of Radiologic Technologist (ARRT)

                    • Annual documentation of ARRT certification and state license required or certification in Cardiovascular Interventional Radiology

                    • Current RT licensure in the state of Delaware required

                    • Current BLS certification required

                    Assistant Nurse Manager Education & Requirements:

                    • Bachelor's degree in nursing required, Master's degree in nursing preferred

                    • Master's degree in nursing required within 5 years of start date.

                    • Three years experience in Interventional Labs : Cardiac Cath, Electrophysiology, Endovascular and/or Neuro required.

                    • Previous leadership experience preferred.

                    • Appropriate specialty certification required.

                    • Current RN licensure in the state of Delaware or licensure accepted to work in the state of Delaware required

                    • Current BLS certification required

                    ChristianaCare Offers:

                    • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.

                    • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions

                    • Generous paid time off with annual roll-over and opportunities to cash out

                    • 12-week paid parental leave

                    • Tuition assistance

                    ]]> Registered Nurse (RN) - Vascular/Neuro Interventional Radiology (VIR)

                    Full Time – Day/Evening

                    On-call requirements

                    Newark & Wilmington Campuses

                    We are currently hiring for a full-time experienced Registered Nurse (RN) to work in the Vascular/Neuro Interventional Radiology Unit within Heart & Vascular Interventional Services at ChristianaCare. RN will work at both the Newark and Wilmington locations.

                    Our VIR team allows ChristianaCare to meet the comprehensive stroke center requirements with our work on HVIS stroke codes.  In addition, we support trauma services with our interventional capabilities.  The RN also supports services at our Wilmington location to allow for continuity of care of those patients as well.  The VIR RN job responsibilities are:

                    • Prep review prior to procedures
                    • Moderate sedation administration
                    • Nursing care of outpatients to critical care patients
                    • Scrub duties to assist the physician.
                    • Anesthesia support as applicable
                    • Monitoring hemodynamics and documentation

                    Vascular Interventional Radiology performed over 5,000 last year.  We perform cutting edge procedures that cater to a wide variety of populations.  Our patient populations range from elective to intensive.  We work closely with multiple specialties, including Vascular Services, Vascular Surgery, Neurology and Nephrology. 

                     Education and Requirements:

                    • BSN required; or commitment to obtaining within three years of date of hire.
                    • BLS and ACLS certifications required.
                    • At least two years of experience in Critical Care setting required.
                    • Moderate sedation experience preferred
                    • Delaware RN licensure or eligibility for licensure in the state of Delaware

                    ChristianaCare Offers:

                    • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.
                    • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions
                    • Generous paid time off with annual roll-over and opportunities to cash-out
                    • 12-week paid parental leave
                    • Tuition assistance
                    • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

                    Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

                    This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    ]]> Patient Registrar I - Floater - Weds., Thurs., Fri., & Sat. - 7a-5:30p

                    PRIMARY FUNCTION:

                    To perform patient registration and scheduling in a busy imaging department and/or outpatient office, including obtaining financial and clinical information, insurance data and authorizations.

                    PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

                    First point of contact interacting with patients

                    Register patients for labs and check in patients efficiently and accurately.

                    Obtains identity, insurance and/or workers compensation information or motor vehicle accident information

                    Answer and route calls into the Outpatient department

                    Responds to questions and concerns and directs them to appropriate location or department.

                    Requests scripts faxed from physician offices to assist patients

                    Documents communication of critical results when necessary

                    Operates computer systems and office equipment in a safe manner.

                    Interacts for solution of “routine problems as they occur to achieve customer satisfaction.

                    Superior performance in customer service and phone skills with ability to manage difficult patient satisfaction issues and provide service recovery.

                    Interacts with patients, family members, physicians, and staff in a courteous and professional manner.

                    Adheres to mandatory education requirements including among attendance, safety, infection control, professionalism, and dress code.

                    Performs assigned work safely, adhering to established departmental safety rules and practices; reports to supervisor, in a timely manner, any unsafe activities, conditions, hazards, or safety violations that may cause injury to oneself, other employees, patients and visitors.

                    Demonstrates Christiana Care values and behaviors.

                    Performs other related duties as required.

                    SCOPE, PURPOSE, AND FREQUENCY OF CONTACTS:

                    Frequent contact with staff, patients, physicians, family members, and third-party insurance to obtain patient information, provide information to client companies, and respond to inquiries concerning patient reports.

                    DIRECTION/SUPERVISION OF OTHERS:

                    None.

                    DIRECTION/SUPERVISION RECEIVED:

                    Patient Access, Supervisor

                    EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

                    High school graduate or equivalent.

                    Two years experience in a medical, financial, or customers service environment.  

                    An equivalent combination of education and experience may be substituted.

                    KNOWLEDGE, SKILL, AND ABILITY REQUIREMENTS:

                    Knowledge of hospital and/or office radiology, clerical and billing procedures/functions including related equipment use and operation.

                    Knowledge of computer scheduling for multiple providers.

                    Knowledge of clerical methods and techniques.

                    Knowledge of office equipment and machines.

                    Knowledge of criteria for safe scheduling parameters (MRI Hazards) when needed.

                    Knowledge of Medical Terminology.

                    Ability to plan, organize, and handle various tasks simultaneously.

                    Ability to function independently, set work schedule and short-term goals to maximize productivity.

                    Ability to establish and maintain superior quality customer relations with patients, patient’s families, Christiana Care staff, visitors, insurance companies, physicians, and the general public.

                    PHYSICAL DEMANDS:

                    Sitting with intermittent standing and walking.  Occasional bending and reaching.  Intermittent light lifting and carrying.

                    WORKING CONDITIONS:

                    Busy office environment.

                    Occasional prolonged sitting and long stretches of time on the phone.

                    Hourly Pay Range: $18.19 - $26.37

                    This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    ]]> Do you want to work at one of the Top 100 Hospitals in the nation? We are guided by our values of Love and Excellence and are passionate about delivering health, not just health care. Come join us at ChristianaCare!

                    Our Pharmacy Department located in Elkton, MD is looking for a full-time Administrative Assistant II to provide administrative support Pharmacy services for the Director of Inpatient Operations This Administrative Assistant will also receive direction from Supervisors, Inpatient Pharmacy. at Union Hospital,

                    The ideal candidate will be well organized, detail oriented and have the ability to multitask. Candidate to complete tasks and duties within a prescribed timeline. Strong computer skills as well as good verbal and written communication skills. This role supports a highly regulated medication use environment that requires frequent interaction with pharmacy operations, compliance documentation, controlled substances, audits, and regulatory agencies. To maintain patient safety, ensure regulatory compliance, and support operational continuity, it is recommended that this AA role be filled by a cross - trained, Maryland- licensed Pharmacy Technician.

                    The adjustment more appropriately aligns the role's responsibility with complexity, regulatory oversight, and the operational demands of inpatient pharmacy services at Union Hospital.

                    Benefits of working at ChristianaCare

                    • Generous PTO, Competitive Pay & Robust Benefits Package.
                    • 403B company match and Tuition Reimbursement
                    • 12 weeks Paid Parental Leave (after one year)

                    The responsibilities of the Administrative Assistant II will include;

                    • Perform duties adhering to the ChristianaCare Core Values and Behaviors guided by Excellence and Love.
                    • Attends required meetings and takes minutes as necessary.
                    • Maintains office supplies for the Department of Pharmacy Services as assigned
                    • Responsible for answering telephones, recording messages; obtains and provides information within scope of authority routing calls to appropriate persons.
                    • Responsible for greeting caregivers and visitors entering the pharmacy administrative suite.
                    • Responsible for scheduling appointments, meetings, and maintains appropriate Outlook calendars for pharmacy Directors and Supervisors as assigned
                    • Schedule interviews prepare itineraries, correspond with applicants
                    • Assures that all purchase orders and receiving information are sent to Accounts Payable for inpatient and outpatient pharmacy in a timely manner as assigned; acts as a liaison between vendor and accounts payable; process annual blanket POs in Strategic Sourcing for invoices
                    • Prepare outpatient deposit checks and send to accounts payable in a timely manner as assigned. Provide administrative support and collaborates with pharmacist partner for the Chemotherapy Regimen Review Committee as assigned
                    • Responsible for ordering uniforms for new hires including assignment of mailbox and lockers.
                    • Distributes departmental mail.
                    • Responsible for entering staff schedules via Kronos Dimensions as assigned.
                    • Responsible for bi-weekly review of automated payroll records and review of FMLA usage reports for staff as assigned via Kronos Dimensions; including documentation of occurrence of absence, late, FMLA (self; caregiver), missed swipes, emergency days, etc.
                    • Responsible for reporting callouts, late arrivals, and emergency days that are reported during office hours via the collaboration site forms, alerting pharmacy Supervisors and Directors, and recording on the call out white board in lower-level pharmacy.
                    • Performs other duties as required.
                    • Perform assigned work safely, adhering to established departmental safety rules and practices. Addresses and/or reports to Vice President/Director(s), in a timely manner, any unsafe activities, conditions, hazards or safety violations that may cause injury to oneself, other employees, patients and visitors.
                    • Performs all pharmacy technician responsibilities on an as needed basis and maintains active licensure for the Maryland State Board of Pharmacy as well as PTCB certifications. (Union only)
                    • Performs other duties as assigned by the Vice President, Pharmacy Services or Directors

                    Office Hours: Day Shift

                    Education and Experience Requirements:

                    • High school graduate
                    • Prior administrative and/or clerical experience and/or courses preferred
                    • Computer knowledge – proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite.
                    • Pharmacy experience preferred
                    • PTCB certifications
                    • Pharmacy Technician License required for State of Maryland (Union ONLY)

                    Knowledge, skill, and ability requirements:

                    • Knowledge of ChristianaCare and departmental policies, procedures, and standards
                    • Knowledge of Joint Commission requirements and standards
                    • Knowledge of computer word processing and data base functions
                    • Skill in oral and written communication
                    • Typing proficiency
                    • Ability to work independently within established procedures
                    • Ability to work well with others in a tactful, courteous, and friendly manner
                    • Ability to practice principles of customer service
                    • Ability to maintain ChristianaCare confidentiality standards
                    • Ability to prioritize work and manage time efficiently
                    • Ability to remain calm in stressful circumstances

                    Physical demands:

                    Sitting with intermittent standing and walking.

                    Repetitive hand movements required to operate PC keyboard and mouse.

                    Computer entry and reading for extended periods of time.

                    Required movement throughout ChristianaCare.

                    May include pushing, pulling, bending. or lifting

                    About ChristianaCare

                    ChristianaCare was recognized as one of "America's 100 Best Hospitals" by Healthgrades, selected as one of the Most Wired Hospitals in the US by the American Hospital Association, and ranked by US News & World Report as the #3 'Best Hospital' in the Philadelphia region out of more than 90 hospitals. To learn more click on this link https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AZtpU0ib3t8

                    We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

                    Hourly Pay Range: $22.29 - $33.44

The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    Posting Job Description

                    Do you want to work at one of the Top 100 Hospitals in the nation? We are guided by our values of Love and Excellence and are passionate about delivering health, not just health care. Come join us at ChristianaCare!

                    ChristianaCare Offers:

                    • Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.

                    • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions.

                    • Generous paid time off with annual roll-over and opportunities to cash out.

                    • 12-week paid parental leave

                    • Tuition assistance

                    • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

                    The Senior Clinical Informatics Specialist serves as a system-wide resource and subject matter expert in clinical informatics. This role promotes evidence-based, best-practice workflows and drives efficiency through the adoption and optimization of clinical information systems. The specialist acts as an advocate for clinicians regarding information technology initiatives, ensuring technology solutions align with the delivery of safe, high-quality, patient-centered care.

                    The incumbent leads and participates in the design, implementation, and evaluation of clinical technologies, with a focus on integration within clinical workflows. Responsibilities include advanced application analysis, Epic system configuration and testing, documentation of system changes, end-user training, and performance improvement support.

                    PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

                    Clinical Informatics Practice

                    • Serve as a liaison between clinical staff, IT, and operational leadership to ensure alignment of clinical workflows with system capabilities.

                    • Lead workflow assessments, identify gaps, and recommend evidence-based solutions to improve safety, efficiency, and user experience.

                    • Participate in and support technology implementations, upgrades, and optimization projects across inpatient, ambulatory, and specialty settings.

                    • Promote clinician engagement, adoption, and satisfaction through education, communication, and usability improvement initiatives.

                    System Configuration and Analysis

                    • Perform advanced application analysis and minor Epic configuration changes in alignment with governance and change control processes.

                    • Develop, execute, and document Epic build testing, system enhancements, and workflow validation.

                    • Maintain proficiency or certification in applicable Epic modules and other clinical systems.

                    Research, Data Management, and Reporting

                    • Research, collect, and analyze operational and clinical information to identify trends and opportunities for improvement.

                    • Develop analytical reports and graphical data presentations illustrating performance outcomes, ROI, and impact measures.

                    • Provide data-driven insights to support leadership decision-making and strategic initiatives.

                    • Collaborate with interdisciplinary teams on performance improvement activities focused on efficiency, quality, and patient experience.

                    Process and Education

                    • Utilize multiple system applications to perform analysis, create reports, and develop training materials for end users.

                    • Incorporate knowledge of ChristianaCare policies and regulatory standards to ensure quality, confidentiality, and safety.

                    • Develop and deliver educational sessions to support clinician competency and promote informatics best practices.

                    EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

                    • Bachelor’s degree in Nursing, Informatics, or other clinical discipline required.

                    • Master’s degree in Informatics, Nursing, or related field preferred.

                    • Minimum of 5 years of experience as a Registered Nurse, Respiratory Therapist, or PharmD in an acute care or ambulatory setting.

                    • Minimum of 2 years of experience in Clinical Informatics or related health IT field.

                    • Experience leading and implementing large, complex healthcare projects required.

                    • Demonstrated ability to adapt to change, manage competing priorities, and think critically in a fast-paced environment.

                    SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS:

                    • Licensure/Certification:

                      • Current Delaware licensure as a Registered Nurse, Respiratory Therapist, or PharmD required.

                      • Epic proficiency or certification in relevant modules preferred.

                      • Vendor-specific application certifications required or obtained within established timeframe.

                    Annual Compensation Range $92,726.40 - $148,387.20

                    This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    Christiana Care Health System is an equal opportunity employer, firmly committed to prohibiting discrimination, whose staff is reflective of its community, and considers qualified applicants for open positions without regard to race, color, sex, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, genetic information, gender identity or expression, age, veteran status, disability, pregnancy, citizenship status, or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law.

                    ]]> Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States.

                    ChristianaCare will not sponsor applicants for this position for an employment visa now or in the future.

                    This role requires weekly on-site work and on-call rotation. Candidates must be within a commutable distance.

                    Why ChristianaCare:

                    Do you want to work at one of the Top 100 Hospitals in the nation? We are guided by our values of Love and Excellence and are passionate about delivering health, not just health care. Come join us at ChristianaCare!

                    ChristianaCare, with Hospitals in Wilmington and Newark, DE, as well as Elkton, MD, is one of the largest health care providers in the Mid-Atlantic Region. Named one of “America’s Best Hospitals by U.S. News & World Report, we have an excess of 1,100 beds between our hospitals and are committed to providing the best patient care in the region. We are proud to that Christiana Hospital, Wilmington Hospital, our Ambulatory Services, and HomeHealth have all received ANCC Magnet Recognition®.

                    ChristianaCare Offers:

                    • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.

                    • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions

                    • Generous paid time off with annual roll-over and opportunities to cash out

                    • 12 week paid parental leave

                    • Tuition assistance

                    • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

                    PRIMARY FUNCTION:

                    The Systems Analyst is responsible for providing support to all our cloud computing needs, including lead, support and monitor infrastructure within public and private cloud environments

                    As System Analyst, you should have a firm grasp of IaaS, PaaS and SaaS technologies, platforms and applications, and have intimate knowledge of how best to customize these attributes to our business to help us become more secure and efficient.

                    Work with key stakeholders in solving operation problems through analyzing workflows, gathering requirements, defining alternatives, recommending and implementing solutions.

                    PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

                    • Installs and maintains cloud-based applications, systems, and associated on-prem infrastructures.
                    • Script and automate routine tasks on Azure/AWS components
                    • Develop documentation, user manuals, and policy and procedure information for AWS/Azure/on-prem architecture
                    • Working in tandem with our engineering team to identify and implement the most efficient cloud-based solutions.
                    • Leading cloud and on-prem environments in accordance with company security guidelines.
                    • Deploying and debugging cloud and on-prem initiatives as needed in accordance with standard methodologies throughout the development lifecycle.
                    • Educating teams on the implementation of new cloud-based initiatives, providing associated training as the need arises.
                    • Employing outstanding problem-solving skills, with the ability to see and tackle issues
                    • Using your extensive knowledge of APIs to craft RESTful services, and integrate them with existing data providers, using JSON or XML as needed.
                    • Experience with AIX, RedHat/Unix systems, monitoring tools like spectrum, Nagios is a plus
                    • Orchestrating and automating cloud-based platforms throughout the company.
                    • Stay ahead of with industry trends, making recommendations as needed to help the company excel.
                    • Lead and develop standard methodologies for cloud and systems Infrastructure team.
                    • Will participate in 24/7 on-call rotation as part of a team supporting various applications.
                    • Define and coordinate the execution of testing procedures and develop test cases\scripts for various applications upgrades or implementations.
                    • Ensure system changes follow change management procedures and protocols.
                    • Provide documentation of all processes and training as needed.

                    MINIMUM EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

                    • BA/BS in Computer Science or related field is preferable. Equivalent combination of experience, education and training will be considered.
                    • Minimum of 2 to 4 years of experience in Information Technology.
                    • One (1) to two (2) years' experience in a Cloud Dev Ops, system role or related position.
                    • One (1) to three (2) years' experience in developing software using languages such as Python, C++.
                    • Azure Certification is a plus.
                    • Healthcare IT experience is preferred but not required.

                    KNOWLEDGE, SKILL, AND ABILITY REQUIREMENTS:

                    • Bachelor’s degree in computer science or engineering; Equivalent combination of experience, education and training will be considered.
                    • Experience with cloud-based services, preferably Azure.
                    • Knowledge/Experience with VMware host upgrades, virtual machine builds, VCF, VROP, deployment, and migration a plus
                    • Knowledge of backups, system manager software distribution, cost optimization and other cloud operational work
                    • Strong awareness of networking and internet protocols, including TCP/IP, DNS, SMTP, HTTP and distributed networks.
                    • Requires attention to detail, pride in delivering excellent service, and high levels of energy and endurance.
                    • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, and the ability to stay focused on completing tasks and meeting goals within a busy workspace.
                    • Skilled at working in tandem with a team of engineers, or alone as the need arises
                    • Experienced in developing, documenting, and executing test scripts preferred.
                    • Up-to-date knowledge of computers, networks, and other information technology tools.
                    • Ability to interact with all levels of healthcare personnel including IT management staff, Healthcare system staff, clinicians Healthcare leadership, etc. via written and/or verbal means.
                    • Ability to manage multiple priorities, assess and adjust quickly to changing priorities.

                    Annual Compensation Range $79,497.60 - $127,212.80

                    This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    We are seeking an experienced Patient Escort III to join our Emergency Department (ED) team. This role is a dedicated transporter position stationed specifically in the ED, supporting timely, safe, and compassionate patient movement throughout the hospital during high‑acuity and fast‑paced situations.

                    The ideal candidate will have prior patient escort or transport experience and thrive in an environment that requires strong communication, teamwork, and professionalism.

                    Location: Christiana - Newark Campus
                    Department: Patient Escort – Emergency Department
                    Schedule: 6:00 AM – 2:30 PM, Requires Every Other Weekend & Every Other Holiday

                    Status: Full-Time, 40 hours per week

                    Why ChristianaCare?

                    • A collaborative, team-focused culture that values your contributions

                    • Comprehensive benefits including Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance

                    • 403(b) retirement plan with employer contributions

                    • Generous paid time off to support work-life balance

                    • Exclusive Work/Life perks:

                      • Annual Care.com membership

                      • Backup care services for dependents

                      • Retirement planning & financial coaching

                      • Wellness reimbursements

                      • Discounts on hotels, rental cars, theme parks, entertainment & more!

                    Primary Function

                    Safely and efficiently transport patients and clinical items to and from the Emergency Department while providing exceptional customer service and support to patients, families, and clinical staff.

                    Key Responsibilities

                    • Transport patients via stretcher, wheelchair, bed, or specialized transport equipment to and from the ED and other hospital departments

                    • Serve as a dedicated member of the Emergency Department care team, responding quickly to transport needs in a fast‑paced environment

                    • Assist ED clinical staff with patient transfers and positioning

                    • Maintain safety by following departmental policies, procedures, and infection prevention guidelines

                    • Pick up and deliver equipment, specimens, medication, and paperwork as appropriate

                    • Assist with care, cleaning, and maintenance of transport equipment

                    • Act as a subject matter expert for patient transport protocols and safety standards

                    • Support dispatch operations as needed

                    • Maintain patient confidentiality and uphold a courteous, service‑oriented attitude at all times

                    • Participate in training, in‑services, and department meetings as required

                    Education & Experience

                    • High school diploma or equivalent required

                    • Previous patient escort or patient transport experience strongly preferred

                    • Minimum of two years of customer service experience

                    • Ability to obtain Basic Life Support (BLS) certification upon hire

                    Required Knowledge, Skills & Abilities

                    • Knowledge of hospital layout and departments (or ability to learn quickly)

                    • Strong verbal communication and customer service skills

                    • Ability to follow written and verbal instructions

                    • Knowledge of basic body mechanics and safe patient handling

                    • Comfort using smartphones and basic computer systems (email, scheduling, timekeeping)

                    • Ability to remain professional, calm, and compassionate in high‑stress situations

                    • Ability to work independently while collaborating closely with ED staff

                    Physical Requirements

                    • Ability to stand and walk for extended periods throughout the shift

                    • Ability to push, pull, lift, and maneuver patients and equipment, sometimes involving considerable weight

                    • Must pass a back evaluation examination

                    Working Conditions

                    • Frequent patient, family, and staff interaction

                    • Exposure to cleaning agents, blood, bodily fluids, and infectious diseases

                    • Frequent handwashing required

                    This position has a starting rate of $18.08 per hour, regardless of prior experience. After six months of continuous employment, the hourly rate will automatically increase to $21.15 per hour. After one year of continuous employment, the hourly rate will automatically increase to $22.18 per hour.

                    This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    Christiana Care Health System is an equal opportunity employer, firmly committed to prohibiting discrimination, whose staff is reflective of its community, and considers qualified applicants for open positions without regard to race, color, sex, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, genetic information, gender identity or expression, age, veteran status, disability, pregnancy, citizenship status, or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law.

                    ]]> ChristianaCare is seeking an accomplished Senior Physician Recruiter to independently manage the complete recruitment process for Physicians, Advanced Practice Clinicians (APCs), and other licensed clinical professionals. This high-impact, visible position is ideal for a seasoned recruiter who excels in navigating complex challenges, building lasting professional relationships, and consistently delivering successful results.

                    Why This Role

                    • Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy the convenience of primarily remote work, allowing you to perform at your best from your preferred location. Occasional in-person site visits and conferences are purposeful and thoughtfully planned.

                    • Full Autonomy: Manage the entire recruitment process independently, including intake, sourcing, screening, offer negotiations, and closing.

                    • High-Level Partnership: Collaborate directly with clinical and operational leaders on impactful positions that support both patient care and organizational growth.

                    Key Responsibilities

                    • Lead end-to-end recruitment for physicians, APCs, and other licensed clinical professionals within the Medical Group.

                    • Conduct intake meetings, develop and execute sourcing strategies, and proactively build diverse and competitive candidate pipelines.

                    • Engage and convert passive candidates through cold calling, social networking, and involvement in professional communities.

                    • Coordinate and enhance both virtual and in-person site visit experiences for candidates.

                    • Work closely with compensation and finance teams to structure offers, negotiate terms, and successfully close hires.

                    • Maintain precise records in Workday to support compliance, affirmative action program activities, and requisition tracking.

                    • Mentor Physician Recruiters, Talent Acquisition Specialists, and Associate TA Specialists, supporting the onboarding of new team members.

                    Qualifications and Expertise

                    • Bachelor’s degree and at least 6 years of full-cycle, high-volume, multi-level recruitment experience, including physician positions.

                    • Demonstrated ability to manage highly competitive searches where candidates are fielding multiple offers.

                    • Expertise in behavioral interviewing, offer negotiation, and candidate influence strategies.

                    • Proven track record of developing innovative and comprehensive sourcing strategies.

                    • Experience with contingency and permanent placement vendor negotiations.

                    • Strong communication skills, including public speaking and executive-level engagement.

                    Ready to Make Your Mark?

                    Join a collaborative Talent Acquisition team at ChristianaCare that values expertise, initiative, and a meaningful impact on clinical hiring. This is your opportunity to contribute to the growth and excellence of healthcare delivery.

                    Annual Compensation Range $85,862.40 - $137,384.00

                    This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    Make a Difference in Critical Care – Join ChristianaCare’s MICU Team!

                    Position: Registered Nurse – Medical Intensive Care Unit
                    Schedule: Full-Time Flex (36 hrs/week) | Night Shift with Day Shift rotations
                    Location: Christiana Hospital

                    Why This Role Matters

                    Are you passionate about saving lives and delivering exceptional care when it matters most? In our Medical Intensive Care Unit, you’ll be at the heart of critical care—where your expertise, compassion, and quick thinking make a real impact every day.

                    This is your opportunity to sharpen your skills, work with advanced technology, and collaborate with a team that values your voice and your commitment to patient advocacy.

                    What You’ll Do

                    • Provide expert care for critically ill patients in a fast-paced, high-acuity environment
                    • Partner with physicians, specialists, and a multidisciplinary team to deliver life-saving interventions
                    • Advocate for patients and families during their most vulnerable moments
                    • Use advanced monitoring and treatment strategies to achieve the best outcomes

                    What Makes You a Great Fit

                    • Strong critical thinking and problem-solving skills
                    • Compassionate, collaborative, and committed to patient advocacy
                    • Thrive under pressure and embrace continuous learning

                    Why ChristianaCare?

                    We offer more than a job—we offer a career that supports your growth and your life:

                    • Competitive pay & comprehensive benefits (Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance)
                    • 403(b) retirement plan with company contributions
                    • Generous PTO with rollover & cash-out options
                    • 12 weeks paid parental leave
                    • Tuition assistance for your professional development
                    • Work/Life perks like Care.com membership, backup care, wellness reimbursements, and discounts on travel, entertainment, and more

                    Our Mission

                    As a nonprofit health system, ChristianaCare is committed to serving our neighbors as expert, caring partners in health. We combine cutting-edge technology with a deep commitment to compassion—because we believe great care starts with great people.

                    Requirements

                    • BSN required (ADN considered with BSN commitment within 3 years)
                    • Minimum 1 year acute care experience required
                    • BLS required; ACLS preferred (must obtain within 6 months of hire)

                    Ready to take your critical care career to the next level? Apply today and join a team that saves lives every day!

                    Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

                    This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    ]]> Registered Nurse (RN) - Wilmington Emergency Department

                    ChristianaCare was named one of “America’s Best Hospitals by U.S. News & World Report and named one of the top 5 Best Large Healthcare Employers by Forbes. This position is located in our Wilmington Hospital. A Wilmington landmark since 1890, our fully modernized Wilmington Hospital serves and provides ChristianaCare’s distinguished, high-quality health care in the heart of Wilmington. Our Wilmington Emergency Department houses a Level III Trauma Center. The 37 bed Wilmington ED serves patients with medical, cardiac and traumatic emergencies. We seek an experienced Registered Nurse for this team. Isn't this the opportunity you've been looking for?

                    Highlights:

                    • Four times recognized as Magnet Status Hospital!
                    • Growth Opportunities defined by our Clinical Ladder.
                    • We offer a robust benefits package. Our healthcare benefits include medical, dental & eyecare, starting day one!  Enjoy generous paid time off, paid parental leave, competitive pay with shift differentials, tuition reimbursement.  Additional benefits include, dependent care assistance, pet insurance, financial coaching, fitness & wellness reimbursements and discounts on multiple services and events.
                    • Work in an Emergency Department with a helipad with aero-medical transport capabilities! The Emergency Department is a participating trauma hospital in the Delaware Trauma System.
                    • 2023 Emergency Nurses Association Lantern Award winner!

                    Requirements:

                    BSN required or commitment to obtaining within three years of date of hire.

                    At least one year of experience in a med/surgical unit or ED required.

                    BLS required. ALCS strongly preferred.

                    Schedule: This position is 7 p.m. - 7:30 a.m. This has a weekend and holiday requirement per company practice.

                    Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

                    This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    ]]> Wilmington Emergency Department - Part Time Day/Evening

                    A Wilmington landmark since 1890, fully modernized Wilmington Hospital serves as ChristianaCare’s corporate headquarters and provides ChristianaCare’s distinguished, high-quality health care in the heart of Wilmington. This is a 321-bed, 622,100-square-foot facility. Wilmington Hospital has a helipad with aero-medical transport capabilities. The Emergency Department houses a Level III Trauma Center and a participating trauma hospital in the Delaware Trauma System.

                    We seek an experienced Registered Nurse for this team. Isn't this the opportunity you've been looking for?

                    Highlights:

                    • Work in an Emergency Department with a helipad with aero-medical transport capabilities! The Emergency Department is a participating trauma hospital in the Delaware Trauma System.
                    • Four times recognized as Magnet Status Hospital!
                    • Growth Opportunities defined by our Clinical Ladder.
                    • You will have the choice between a higher incentive rate or the option to select benefits. We offer a robust benefits package. Additional benefits include, dependent care assistance, pet insurance, financial coaching, fitness & wellness reimbursements and discounts on multiple services and events.

                    Requirements:

                    BSN required or commitment to obtaining within three years of date of hire.

                    At least one year of experience in a med/surgical unit or ED required.

                    BLS required. ALCS strongly preferred.

                    Hours: The hours can be 7 a.m. - 7:30 p.m. or 11 a.m. - 11:30 p.m. This is for 48 hours per pay/24 hours per week. This offers a weekend schedule.

                    Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

                    ]]> Are you looking to work for a company that is based on excellence and love? Then this is the position you need to check out!

                    ChristianaCare is one of the country's most dynamic healthcare organizations, centered on improving health outcomes, making high-quality care more accessible, and lowering healthcare costs. ChristianaCare includes an extensive network of outpatient services, home health care, urgent care centers, three hospitals (1,299 beds), a free-standing emergency department, a Level I trauma center and a Level III neonatal intensive care unit, a comprehensive stroke center and regional centers of excellence in heart and vascular care, cancer care, and women's health. It also includes the pioneering Gene Editing Institute and was rated by IDG Computerworld as one of the nation's Best Places to Work in IT. ChristianaCare is a nonprofit teaching health system with more than 260 residents and fellows. It is continually ranked by U.S. News & World Report as the Best Hospital. With the unique CareVio data-powered care coordination service and a focus on population health and value-based care, ChristianaCare is shaping the future of health care.

                    ChristianaCare Offers

                    • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.

                    • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions

                    • Generous paid time off with annual roll-over and opportunities to cash out

                    • 12-week paid parental leave

                    • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

                    About This Position

                    We are seeking a Full-Time HR Service Representative who will primarily be responsible for responding to general inquiries and concerns from employees and management demonstrating an in-depth knowledge of Human Resources, Payroll and Expense policies and practices in a professional and courteous manner.

                    Principal Duties and Responsibilities:

                    • Responds accurately to a high volume of calls demonstrating outstanding customer service skills.

                    • Receives, investigates and responds to all customer inquiries using knowledge base system.

                    • Conducts customer call backs within established time frames or provide update on delays.

                    • Logs detailed and accurate information on calls received in the case management system.

                    • Interprets and responds clearly and effectively to all verbal or written requests for information.

                    • Demonstrates sound judgment in escalating employee relations or other HR inquiries to the appropriate content experts.

                    • Utilizes CCHS heat methodology when service issues arise.

                    • Stays current on recent federal, state and case law changes through monitoring labor law updates that impact organizations policies and procedures.

                    • Accesses and proactively recommend solutions when repetitions of individual problems indicate negative trends.

                    • Conducts exit interviews with all role model staff within established time frames to assist in resolving if corrective action may retain valued staff members.

                    Education and Experience Requirements:

                    • High school diploma required (Bachelor’s degree preferred).
                    • Two years’ customer service and Human Resources experience required.
                    • Previous call center experienced preferred.
                    • An equivalent combination of education and experience may be substituted

                    Hourly Pay Range: $22.29 - $33.44

                    This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    Christiana Care Health System is an equal opportunity employer, firmly committed to prohibiting discrimination, whose staff is reflective of its community, and considers qualified applicants for open positions without regard to race, color, sex, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, genetic information, gender identity or expression, age, veteran status, disability, pregnancy, citizenship status, or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law.

                    ]]> Do you want to work at one of the top 100 hospitals in the nation?

                    Life is full of choices, and whom you work for is one of life's most important decisions. ChristianaCare is ranked as one of the top 100 hospitals in the nation and has earned national recognition for excellence. Choose an organization that provides career advancement and the opportunity to impact health.

                    Christiana Care is seeking a full time Occupational Therapist to work with a dynamic team in this patient centered facility. Patient care includes but is not limited to the coordination of acute care services at the ChristianaCare Newark campus.  This exciting position allows you to see a large variety of patient populations including Neuro, Trauma, Cardiac, Orthopedic and General Debility while working with a supportive team of OTs. The OT experience at Christiana Care Health System offers a strong team approach, frequent interdisciplinary interaction, ongoing mentoring, state of the art equipment and professional level educational opportunities.

                    Education and experience requirements:

                    • BS, MS, or Doctorate in Occupational Therapy
                    • At least two years of clinical experience preferred
                    • Licensure or license eligibility in the State of Delaware as an Occupational Therapist
                    • CPR Certification
                    • AOTA preferred

                    ChristianaCare offers:

                    Incredible Work/Life benefits including medical/dental/ vision insurance coverage on your first day of employment, paid PTO, retirement plans, twelve weeks of paid parental leave, annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

                    ]]> Are you an experienced marketing professional looking for a phenomenal career opportunity joining a company based on excellence and love?

                    ChristianaCare is one of the country's most dynamic healthcare organizations, centered on improving health outcomes, making high-quality care more accessible, and lowering healthcare costs. ChristianaCare includes an extensive network of outpatient services, home health care, urgent care centers, three hospitals (1,430 beds), a free-standing emergency department, a Level I trauma center and a Level III neonatal intensive care unit, a comprehensive stroke center and regional centers of excellence in heart and vascular care, cancer care, and women's health. It also includes the pioneering Gene Editing Institute and was rated by IDG Computerworld as one of the nation's Best Places to Work in IT. ChristianaCare is a nonprofit teaching health system with more than 260 residents and fellows. It is also continually ranked among the nation’s best hospitals by U.S. News & World Report!

                    ChristianaCare Offers

                    • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.
                    • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions
                    • Generous paid time off with annual roll-over and opportunities to cash out
                    • 12-week paid parental leave
                    • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial coaching, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

                    About This Position

                    ChristianaCare is searching for a Senior Marketing Specialist to develop and execute strategic marketing plans for the purposes of patient acquisition and patient retention within ChristianaCare, its subsidiaries or joint ventures. With a heavy-focus on evidence-based practices and ROI, the Specialist will direct paid and organic strategies for a variety of clinical services, practices, and/or programs in accordance with ChristianaCare’s operating objectives.

                    Responsibilities include...

                    • Develops strategic data-driven growth and marketing plans that align with overall ChristianaCare’s goals.
                    • Advises clinical and operational leaders on marketing strategy and opportunities for external awareness and patient growth.
                    • Collaborates with colleagues within the department and throughout ChristianaCare, securing appropriate input, reviews, and approvals to ensure that the plan is executed to meet marketing needs, overall system goals and corporate brand.
                    • Oversees external advertising campaigns and coordinates efforts with relevant partners to establish and maintain consistency between media or delegates appropriate projects to Marketing Coordinators.
                    • Develops strategy to engage with referring providers and other external stakeholders to grow business.
                    • Identifies needs within the network of employed, affiliated, and unaffiliated providers that can be addressed through marketing efforts.
                    • Develops and maintains a detailed assessment of the competitive landscape for ChristianaCare’s primary and secondary service areas.
                    • Creates a strategic plan for periodic market research assessments to fully understand patient demographics and provider/referral pipeline.
                    • Initiates, plans, organizes, and manages a variety of projects within the scope of responsibility to meet the needs of internal clients and to coordinate with ChristianaCare’s overall marketing strategy.
                    • Manages marketing meetings with appropriate leaders and provides regular progress reports.
                    • Develops comprehensive annual budget for clients by partnering with internal and external stakeholders.
                    • Follows procedures regarding saving and archiving work to create accurate, useful records for entire department.
                    • Provides superior client support for internal stakeholders.
                    • Oversees development of advertising creative and campaign strategy with internal and external partners.
                    • Makes recommendations on ways to improve patient acquisition, brand identity and messaging. 
                    • Develops process of creating and delivering KPI reports on all campaigns and marketing efforts, in partnership with internal stakeholders and external vendors.
                    • Performs assigned work safely, adhering to established departmental safety rules and practices; reports to supervisor, in a timely manner, any unsafe activities, conditions, hazards, or safety violations that may cause injury to oneself, other employees, patients and visitors.
                    • Performs other related duties as required.

                    Education and Experience Requirements

                    • Bachelor's Degree required. Degree in Marketing preferred.
                    • Ten years or more of marketing experience, with a demonstrated ability to think critically and successfully develop multi-faceted marketing plans using internal and external resources such as advertising agencies, writers and designers, printers, photographers.
                    • Healthcare experience preferred.
                    • Understanding of digital marketing is preferred.
                    • Experience in sales pipeline management and/or business development preferred.
                    • An equivalent combination of education and experience may be substituted

                    Annual Compensation Range $79,497.60 - $127,212.80

                    This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    ]]> Is working with an encouraging team important to you? This is your chance to be a part of an outstanding team of healthcare professionals.

                    Christiana Care Health System is seeking a Registered Nurse to work 18 hours per week on the Night Shift at our Christiana Hospital on the 3D Transitional Stepdown Unit. Weekend and Holidays - per the guidelines of the department.

                    The Transitional Medical Unit (TMU)/3D Intermediate Unit team offers an unique opportunity to provide care for acutely ill patients requiring different levels of care. AACN Beacon Award Recipient, Bronze and Silver

                    Responsibilities:

                    Utilize critical thinking and clinical expertise to assess, plan, and deliver individualized care to an adult patient population with a respiratory focus. As a member of our team, you will work closely with the interdisciplinary team including nursing, social work, pharmacy, respiratory therapist, hospitalists, and consultants such as Pulmonologists, Nephrologists, Cardiologists, and Infectious Disease.

                    A range of diagnoses including ventilator dependent respiratory failure, COPD, Pulmonary Hypertension, Acute Renal Failure, GI Bleed, DKA, Sepsis, and many others. TMU/3C nurses are highly skilled in caring for acutely ill and complex patients, who are proficient in ventilator management, trach care, suctioning, wound care, and more.

                    We deliver comprehensive patient care that is patient and family centered to ensure patient comfort and positive outcomes by providing education related to medications and disease process. In addition, you will closely monitor patients and manage any emergencies than may arise. Once stabilized, we assist with the transition of patients to the appropriate level of care. We assist in managing 3-4 patient assignment.

                    We offer a variety of part time and full time positions and pride ourselves with flexible scheduling to meet the individual needs of our team members. The leadership team to devoted to providing professional advancement opportunities for our staff, such as guiding our nurses to become a preceptor, charge RN, member of unit and/or system council, and certified.

                    We encourage board certification in Medical-Surgical, Geriatric, Progressive Care, and/or Critical Care Nursing.

                    Qualities & Experience We are Looking For:

                    Excellent communication & prioritization skills

                    Ability to work effectively and professionally with an interdisciplinary team and peers

                    A positive outlook and willingness to embrace change

                    ChristianaCare Offers:

                    • Full Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, etc.
                    • Two retirement planning offerings, including 403(b) with company contributions
                    • Generous paid time off with annual roll-over and opportunities to cash out
                    • 12 week paid parental leave
                    • Tuition assistance
                    • Incredible Work/Life benefits including annual membership to care.com, access to backup care services for dependents through Care@Work, retirement planning services, financial mentor, fitness and wellness reimbursement, and great discounts through several vendors for hotels, rental cars, theme parks, shows, sporting events, movie tickets and much more!

                    What are we about: Our mission as an organization is to serve our neighbors as expert, caring partners in their health. We do this by creating innovative, effective, affordable systems of care that our neighbors value. This is our promise to you. As a nonprofit health system, our mission is one of service. We believe that the key to providing truly great health care is to partner with our patients and their families, building a system of care that is effective, affordable and valuable to everyone who is touched by it.

                    ChristianaCare has the most advanced health care technology available to help meet your needs. But more important than the technology is our commitment to partner with you to meet your individual needs. We want to care for you and help you live the healthy life you want to live.

                    General Requirements:

                    • BSN required.
                    • ADNs candidate are required to commitment to obtaining BSN within three years of date of hire can be considered with relevant experience.
                    • Qualified applicants will have at least one year experience. Acute care or sub acute care experience preferred.
                    • BLS required

                    Hourly Pay Range: $41.28 - $66.05

                    This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    ]]> ChristianaCare Health System is seeking a Nurse Manager for our Behavioral Health and CIS Units. The Nurse Manager will lead all aspects of the Behavioral Health and CIS Unit.  In this role, the Nurse Manager will:

                    • Demonstrate knowledge and skills necessary to provide care to adult and geriatric patients, including the ability to obtain and interpret information to identify patient needs.

                    • Assure compliance with regulatory and professional standards.

                    • Identify priorities of management responsibilities and demonstrates the ability to delegate.

                    • Develop and maintain method to ensure patient satisfaction on assigned unit.

                    • Perform routine patient rounds to establish visibility, teamwork, and monitoring the level of care/practice.

                    • Remain cognizant of risk management issues and takes appropriate measures to correct.

                    • Support and communicate Department of Nursing and unit-based PI initiatives.

                    • Lead nursing practice and care delivery in designated unit.

                    • Promote the autonomy of the nursing staff, encouraging independent decision making and problem solving.

                    • Support and maintain shared decision-making on unit.

                    Experience & Education Requirements:

                    • Completed BSN and Master’s degree or alternatively, a Bachelor’s degree and Masters of Science in Nursing required.

                    • Current RN licensure accepted in the state of Maryland required.

                    • BLS Certification required.

                    • 5 years appropriate clinical experience in acute care setting required.

                    • Experience in Critical Care experience strongly preferred

                    • 5 years of appropriate leadership experience required

                    • Professional certification in a related nursing specialty required.

                    • Nurse Executive certification preferred

                    ]]> ChristianaCare Rehabilitation Department is recruiting for a Rehabilitation Aide to work full time. Hours are Monday through Friday, 8:00 to 4:30. One to two weekends days per month required and two holidays per year required.

                    As the Rehabilitation Aide, you will provide clinical and administrative support services in the Physical Therapy/Occupational Therapy, and Recreational Therapy units within the Rehabilitation Services department.   

                    PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

                    • Assist the therapists in treatment preparation for group or individual sessions.  Sessions include, but are not limited to, hot and cold packs, routine follow-up exercises, set-up of therapeutic modality interventions, ambulation, and functional training.
                    • Maintain the work area in a neat and orderly fashion, safe for patient care.  Clean and prepare equipment for patient use.
                    • Maintain inventory of patient supplies and equipment including wheelchairs and their adaptations (where applicable).
                    • Assist therapists in medical equipment/ supply ordering.
                    • Monitor and help to maintain efficient patient flow within the department; assisting with scheduling and patient transport within the department.
                    • Demonstrate skills and knowledge necessary to provide care appropriate to adolescent, adult, and geriatric patients, including knowledge of growth and development, the ability to obtain and interpret information to identify patient needs, and to provide the care needed.
                    • Submit work orders as needed to clinical engineering and facilities.
                    • Assist clerical staff with clerical duties including, but not limited to, scheduling, faxing, filing, answering phones, and scanning.
                    • Performs other related duties as required.

                    EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

                    • High School graduate or equivalent. 
                    • Experience in working with adult physical disabilities preferred
                    • Knowledge of routine physical, occupational, and recreational procedures
                    • Knowledge of correct cleaning and infection control procedures
                    • Knowledge of basic medical terminology.

                    Hourly Pay Range: $18.75 - $22.91

                    This pay rate/range represents ChristianaCare’s good faith and reasonable estimate of compensation at the time of posting. The actual salary within this range offered to a successful candidate will depend on individual factors including without limitation skills, relevant experience, and qualifications as they relate to specific job requirements.

                    ]]> Life is full of choices, and whom you work for is one of life's most important decisions. ChristianaCare is ranked as one of the top 100 hospitals in the nation and has earned national recognition for excellence. Choose an organization that provides career advancement and the opportunity to impact health.

                    ChristianaCare's Center for Rehabilitation at Wilmington Hospital has been recognized by Newsweek's list of America's Best Physical Rehabilitation Centers, ranking ChristianaCare as the number one rehabilitation center in Delaware.

                    The Center for Rehabilitation’s innovative facility includes state-of-the-art equipment, the latest therapeutic technology and techniques, and real-life skill simulation environments, focusing on helping people increase their strength and skills as they prepare to return home.  

                    Christiana Care Health System is seeking a Physical Therapist to work at the Center for Rehabilitation at Wilmington Hospital. This position is full time and will work a Tuesday through Saturday schedule.

                    As a Physical Therapist with Christiana Care, you will be part of a dynamic team which includes treating patients and providing therapy for individuals requiring rehabilitation after injury or disease process. You will establish appropriate treatment programs that include goals to be attained and complete all documentation relating to patient care, including evaluations, progress notes, and discharge summaries. Our skilled team of therapists has developed many special skills that enhance our care of the patients we serve. We encourage active learning and have a dynamic student affiliation program.

                    Education and Experience Requirements:

                    • Bachelors, Masters or Doctorate degree in Physical Therapy
                    • CPR Certification required
                    • Current Delaware license or eligibility for a Delaware License
                    • New graduates are encouraged to apply

                    ChristianaCare’s Center for Rehabilitation at Wilmington Hospital delivers personalized patient care that addresses each patient’s distinct needs. The center specializes in assisting patients and their families as they recover from amputation, brain injury, cancer complications, fractures, orthopedic conditions, joint replacement, neurologic disorders, spinal cord injuries, stroke, and trauma.  ​
